### 3. Password-Auth Callback & SSH Context Plumbing

**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/server/auth_middleware.go`

    ctxKeyUserID ctxKey = "user_id"      // int64; set on real login

**`PasswordAuth` callback (lines 22–43):**
- Detects `ctx.User() == auth.ReservedUsernameNew` → sets `ctxKeyRegister = true` and returns `true` (allows entry) (lines 30–32)
- Otherwise calls `auth.VerifyLogin(context.Background(), st, ctx.User(), password, host)` (line 34)
- On success: sets `ctxKeyUserID = user.ID` (line 39)
- On failure: logs and returns `false` (line 37)
- Always extracts `RemoteAddr()` and stashes via `ctx.SetValue(ctxKeyRemoteHost, ...)` (lines 25–28)

**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/server/server.go`

**`makeProgramHandler` routing (lines 41–96):**
- Checks `ctx.Value(ctxKeyRegister).(bool)` — if true, creates `newRegisterModel(deps)` with `IsRegister: true` (lines 46–49)

- Registers session into broker for live water-balloon delivery (line 69)
- Drains unread water balloons from DB and replays as toasts (lines 75–85)

**Fallthrough:** If no `user_id` in context (neither register mode nor real login), session is rejected (lines 53–56).

1	-- Initial schema for the SSH BBS.
2	-- All tables use auto-increment INTEGER PRIMARY KEY plus a unique business
3	-- key where appropriate. We deliberately do NOT mimic pttbbs's recno-based
4	-- addressing (a holdover from mmap'd binary .DIR files) — a relational DB
5	-- removes the recno-tombstone fragility documented in pttbbs.
6	
7	PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
8	
9	CREATE TABLE users (
10	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
11	    user_id         TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- userec_t.userid
12	    password_hash   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- bcrypt; replaces userec_t.passwd (DES)
13	    nickname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.nickname
14	    realname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.realname
15	    email           TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.email
16	    num_logins      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numlogindays (loose)
17	    num_posts       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numposts
18	    last_login_at   DATETIME,                            -- userec_t.lastlogin
19	    last_host       TEXT,                                -- userec_t.lasthost
20	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
21	);
22	
23	CREATE TABLE boards (
24	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
25	    name            TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- boardheader_t.brdname
26	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- boardheader_t.title
27	    description     TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
28	    bm              TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- boardheader_t.BM (comma-sep)
29	    attr            INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- boardheader_t.brdattr (P1+)
30	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
31	);
32	
33	CREATE TABLE articles (
34	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
35	    board_id        INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES boards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
36	    author_id       INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
37	    author_userid   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized (fileheader_t.owner)
38	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- fileheader_t.title
39	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
40	    recommend_score INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- cached sum of pushes (+1) - boos (-1)
41	    filemode        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- fileheader_t.filemode (P1+)
42	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
43	);
44	CREATE INDEX idx_articles_board_created ON articles(board_id, created_at DESC);
45	
46	CREATE TABLE pushes (
47	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
48	    article_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES articles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
49	    user_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
50	    user_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
51	    kind            TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (kind IN ('push','boo','arrow')), -- 推/噓/→
52	    body            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
53	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
54	);
55	CREATE INDEX idx_pushes_article ON pushes(article_id, id);
56	
57	CREATE TABLE water_balloons (
58	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
59	    from_user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
60	    from_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
61	    to_user_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
62	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
63	    delivered_live  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
64	    read_at         DATETIME,
65	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
66	);
67	CREATE INDEX idx_wb_to_unread ON water_balloons(to_user_id, read_at);
68
```

```text
1	package auth
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"fmt"
7		"regexp"
8		"strings"
9	
10		"golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
13	)
14	
15	// ReservedUsernameNew is the SSH username that triggers the in-TUI register
16	// flow instead of a real DB login.
17	const ReservedUsernameNew = "new"
18	
19	const (
20		bcryptCost     = 12
21		minPasswordLen = 6
22		maxPasswordLen = 128
23		maxNickname    = 32
24		maxEmail       = 128
25	)
26	
27	var (
28		ErrUserExists       = errors.New("user already exists")
29		ErrInvalidUserID    = errors.New("user id must be 3-12 chars, start with a letter, and use only letters/digits/underscore")
30		ErrInvalidPassword  = fmt.Errorf("password must be %d-%d characters", minPasswordLen, maxPasswordLen)
31		ErrNicknameTooLong  = fmt.Errorf("nickname must be %d characters or fewer", maxNickname)
32		ErrEmailTooLong     = fmt.Errorf("email must be %d characters or fewer", maxEmail)
33		ErrReservedUsername = fmt.Errorf("user id %q is reserved", ReservedUsernameNew)
34		ErrBadCredentials   = errors.New("invalid user id or password")
35	)
36	
37	var userIDRe = regexp.MustCompile(`^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_]{2,11}$`)
38	
39	// Register validates inputs, hashes the password with bcrypt, and inserts a
40	// new user. Returns ErrUserExists on UNIQUE conflict.
41	func Register(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, userID, password, nickname, email string) (*store.User, error) {
42		userID = strings.TrimSpace(userID)
43		nickname = strings.TrimSpace(nickname)
44		email = strings.TrimSpace(email)
45	
46		if !userIDRe.MatchString(userID) {
47			return nil, ErrInvalidUserID
48		}
49		if strings.EqualFold(userID, ReservedUsernameNew) {
50			return nil, ErrReservedUsername
51		}
52		if l := len(password); l < minPasswordLen || l > maxPasswordLen {
53			return nil, ErrInvalidPassword
54		}
55		if len([]rune(nickname)) > maxNickname {
56			return nil, ErrNicknameTooLong
57		}
58		if len(email) > maxEmail {
59			return nil, ErrEmailTooLong
60		}
61	
62		hash, err := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(password), bcryptCost)
63		if err != nil {
64			return nil, fmt.Errorf("hash password: %w", err)
65		}
66	
67		u, err := st.Users().Create(ctx, userID, string(hash), nickname, email)
68		if err != nil {
69			// modernc.org/sqlite returns a wrapped UNIQUE error; check the text.
70			if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "UNIQUE") {
71				return nil, ErrUserExists
72			}
73			return nil, err
74		}
75		return u, nil
76	}
77	
78	// VerifyLogin returns the user record if the password matches, ErrBadCredentials otherwise.
79	// On success, NoteLogin is called to update last_login_at / last_host / num_logins.
80	func VerifyLogin(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, userID, password, host string) (*store.User, error) {
81		u, err := st.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, strings.TrimSpace(userID))
82		if err != nil {
83			if errors.Is(err, store.ErrUserNotFound) {
84				// Run a dummy bcrypt check anyway to even out timing.
85				_ = bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte("$2a$12$invalidinvalidinvalidinvalidinvalidinvalidinvalidinvali"), []byte(password))
86				return nil, ErrBadCredentials
87			}
88			return nil, err
89		}
90		if err := bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte(u.PasswordHash), []byte(password)); err != nil {
91			return nil, ErrBadCredentials
92		}
93		if err := st.Users().NoteLogin(ctx, u.ID, host); err != nil {
94			return nil, fmt.Errorf("note login: %w", err)
95		}
96		// Re-fetch so callers see the updated last_login_at / num_logins.
97		return st.Users().GetByID(ctx, u.ID)
98	}
99
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/server/auth_middleware.go`

```text
1	package server
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5	
6		"github.com/charmbracelet/log"
7		"github.com/charmbracelet/ssh"
8	
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/auth"
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
11	)
12	
13	// Context keys for values stashed by PasswordAuth and read by teaHandler.
14	type ctxKey string
15	
16	const (
17		ctxKeyUserID     ctxKey = "user_id"      // int64; set on real login
18		ctxKeyRegister   ctxKey = "register"     // bool; true if SSH user == "new"
19		ctxKeyRemoteHost ctxKey = "remote_host"  // string; client IP
20	)
21	
22	func PasswordAuth(st *store.Store) ssh.PasswordHandler {
23		return func(ctx ssh.Context, password string) bool {
24			host := ""
25			if a := ctx.RemoteAddr(); a != nil {
26				host = a.String()
27			}
28			ctx.SetValue(ctxKeyRemoteHost, host)
29	
30			if ctx.User() == auth.ReservedUsernameNew {
31				ctx.SetValue(ctxKeyRegister, true)
32				return true
33			}
34			user, err := auth.VerifyLogin(context.Background(), st, ctx.User(), password, host)
35			if err != nil {
36				log.Info("auth failed", "user", ctx.User(), "host", host, "err", err)
37				return false
38			}
39			ctx.SetValue(ctxKeyUserID, user.ID)
40			log.Info("auth ok", "user", user.UserID, "host", host)
41			return true
42		}
43	}
44
```
</details></tool-use>

1	package server
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5	
6		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
7		"github.com/charmbracelet/log"
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/ssh"
9		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish"
10		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/activeterm"
11		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/bubbletea"
12		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/logging"
13		"github.com/muesli/termenv"
14	
15		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
16		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
17		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/tui"
18	)
19	
20	type Config struct {
21		Addr    string
22		HostKey string
23	}
24	
25	func New(cfg Config, st *store.Store, broker *chat.Broker) (*ssh.Server, error) {
26		return wish.NewServer(
27			wish.WithAddress(cfg.Addr),
28			wish.WithHostKeyPath(cfg.HostKey),
29			wish.WithPasswordAuth(PasswordAuth(st)),
30			wish.WithMiddleware(
31				bubbletea.MiddlewareWithProgramHandler(makeProgramHandler(st, broker), termenv.ANSI256),
32				activeterm.Middleware(),
33				logging.Middleware(),
34			),
35		)
36	}
37	
38	// makeProgramHandler is the wish ProgramHandler. It owns *tea.Program
39	// creation so we can hand the pointer to the chat broker for live message
40	// delivery, and arrange Unregister when the SSH session ends.
41	func makeProgramHandler(st *store.Store, broker *chat.Broker) bubbletea.ProgramHandler {
42		return func(sess ssh.Session) *tea.Program {
43			ctx := sess.Context()
44	
45			// Register-mode: SSH user was "new". No DB user, no broker registration.
46			if reg, ok := ctx.Value(ctxKeyRegister).(bool); ok && reg {
47				deps := tui.Deps{Store: st, Broker: broker, IsRegister: true}
48				m := tui.NewRoot(deps)
49				return tea.NewProgram(m, append(bubbletea.MakeOptions(sess), tea.WithAltScreen())...)
50			}
51	
52			uid, _ := ctx.Value(ctxKeyUserID).(int64)
53			if uid == 0 {
54				log.Warn("session has no user_id; rejecting", "remote", sess.RemoteAddr())
55				return nil
56			}
57			user, err := st.Users().GetByID(context.Background(), uid)
58			if err != nil {
59				log.Error("load user", "id", uid, "err", err)
60				return nil
61			}
62	
63			deps := tui.Deps{Store: st, User: user, Broker: broker}
64			m := tui.NewRoot(deps)
65			p := tea.NewProgram(m, append(bubbletea.MakeOptions(sess), tea.WithAltScreen())...)
66	
67			// Hook session into the broker so other users can send to us.
68			cs := &chat.Session{UserID: user.ID, UserIDStr: user.UserID, Program: p}
69			broker.Register(cs)
70			log.Info("session registered", "user", user.UserID, "online", len(broker.OnlineList()))
71	
72			// Drain unread water balloons from DB and replay them as toasts.
73			// We do this *before* the program starts so the messages land in
74			// the model's first Update(); tea.Program.Send buffers if needed.
75			go func() {
76				unread, err := st.WaterBalloons().ListUnreadFor(context.Background(), user.ID)
77				if err != nil {
78					log.Warn("drain unread WBs", "user", user.UserID, "err", err)
79					return
80				}
81				for _, w := range unread {
82					p.Send(tui.WBIncomingMsg{ID: w.ID, FromUserID: w.FromUserIDStr, Body: w.Body})
83					_ = st.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), w.ID)
84				}
85			}()
86	
87			// On session end, unregister so we stop trying to deliver to a dead Program.
88			go func() {
89				<-ctx.Done()
90				broker.Unregister(user.ID, p)
91				log.Info("session unregistered", "user", user.UserID, "online", len(broker.OnlineList()))
92			}()
93	
94			return p
95		}
96	}
97
```

3	import (

14	const (
15		regFieldUserID = iota
16		regFieldPassword
17		regFieldNickname
18		regFieldEmail
19		regFieldCount
20	)
21	
22	type registerModel struct {
23		deps    Deps
24		inputs  []textinput.Model
25		focus   int
26		err     string
27		success bool
28		done    string // success: registered userid
29	}
30	
31	func newRegisterModel(deps Deps) registerModel {
32		inputs := make([]textinput.Model, regFieldCount)
33	
34		inputs[regFieldUserID] = textinput.New()
35		inputs[regFieldUserID].Placeholder = "alice"
36		inputs[regFieldUserID].CharLimit = 12
37		inputs[regFieldUserID].Width = 32
38		inputs[regFieldUserID].Focus()
39	
40		inputs[regFieldPassword] = textinput.New()
41		inputs[regFieldPassword].Placeholder = "≥ 6 characters"
42		inputs[regFieldPassword].EchoMode = textinput.EchoPassword
43		inputs[regFieldPassword].EchoCharacter = '•'
44		inputs[regFieldPassword].CharLimit = 128
45		inputs[regFieldPassword].Width = 32
46	
47		inputs[regFieldNickname] = textinput.New()
48		inputs[regFieldNickname].Placeholder = "愛麗絲"
49		inputs[regFieldNickname].CharLimit = 64
50		inputs[regFieldNickname].Width = 40
51	
52		inputs[regFieldEmail] = textinput.New()
53		inputs[regFieldEmail].Placeholder = "alice@example.com"
54		inputs[regFieldEmail].CharLimit = 128
55		inputs[regFieldEmail].Width = 40
56	
57		return registerModel{deps: deps, inputs: inputs}
58	}
59	
60	func (m registerModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
61	
62	func (m registerModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
63		switch msg := msg.(type) {
64		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
65			return m, nil
66	
67		case tea.KeyMsg:
68			if m.success {
69				return m, tea.Quit
70			}
71			switch msg.String() {
72			case "esc":
73				return m, tea.Quit
74			case "tab", "down":
75				m.advance(+1)
76				return m, nil
77			case "shift+tab", "up":
78				m.advance(-1)
79				return m, nil
80			case "enter":
81				if m.focus < len(m.inputs)-1 {
82					m.advance(+1)
83					return m, nil
84				}
85				return m.submit()
86			}
87		}
88	
89		var cmd tea.Cmd
90		m.inputs[m.focus], cmd = m.inputs[m.focus].Update(msg)
91		return m, cmd
92	}
93	
94	func (m *registerModel) advance(d int) {
95		n := len(m.inputs)
96		m.focus = (m.focus + d + n) % n
97		for i := range m.inputs {
98			if i == m.focus {
99				m.inputs[i].Focus()
100			} else {
101				m.inputs[i].Blur()
102			}
103		}
104	}
105	
106	func (m registerModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
107		user, err := auth.Register(context.Background(), m.deps.Store,
108			m.inputs[regFieldUserID].Value(),
109			m.inputs[regFieldPassword].Value(),
110			m.inputs[regFieldNickname].Value(),
111			m.inputs[regFieldEmail].Value(),
112		)
113		if err != nil {
114			m.err = err.Error()
115			return m, nil
116		}
117		m.success = true
118		m.done = user.UserID
119		return m, nil
120	}
121	
122	func (m registerModel) View() string {
123		if m.success {
124			return StyleSuccess.Render("\n  ✓ 註冊成功！") +
125				"\n\n  請重新連線：\n\n    " +
126				StyleHighlight.Render(fmt.Sprintf("ssh %s@<host> -p <port>", m.done)) +
127				"\n\n  按任意鍵結束。\n"
128		}
129	
130		var b strings.Builder
131		b.WriteString("\n")
132		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render("  === 註冊新帳號 ==="))
133		b.WriteString("\n\n")
134	
135		labels := []string{
136			"帳號 user id (3-12, 字母開頭, 英數+底線)",
137			"密碼 password (≥ 6)",
138			"暱稱 nickname",
139			"Email (optional)",
140		}
141		for i, label := range labels {
142			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(label))
143			b.WriteString("\n  ")
144			b.WriteString(m.inputs[i].View())
145			b.WriteString("\n\n")
146		}
147	
148		if m.err != "" {
149			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n\n")
150		}
151		b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab/↓ next · Shift+Tab/↑ prev · Enter submit · Esc/Ctrl+C cancel") + "\n")
152		return b.String()
153	}

 `./internal/store/users.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type User struct {
11		ID            int64
12		UserID        string
13		PasswordHash  string
14		Nickname      string
15		RealName      string
16		Email         string
17		NumLogins     int64
18		NumPosts      int64
19		LastLoginAt   sql.NullTime
20		LastHost      sql.NullString
21		CreatedAt     time.Time
22	}
23	
24	type UserRepo struct{ s *Store }
25	
26	var ErrUserNotFound = errors.New("user not found")
27	
28	const userColumns = `id, user_id, password_hash, nickname, realname, email,
29		num_logins, num_posts, last_login_at, last_host, created_at`
30	
31	func scanUser(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*User, error) {
32		u := &User{}
33		err := row.Scan(
34			&u.ID, &u.UserID, &u.PasswordHash, &u.Nickname, &u.RealName, &u.Email,
35			&u.NumLogins, &u.NumPosts, &u.LastLoginAt, &u.LastHost, &u.CreatedAt,
36		)
37		return u, err
38	}
39	
40	func (r *UserRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, userID, passwordHash, nickname, email string) (*User, error) {
41		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
42		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
43		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
44			`INSERT INTO users (user_id, password_hash, nickname, email) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
45			userID, passwordHash, nickname, email,
46		)
47		if err != nil {
48			return nil, err
49		}
50		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
51		if err != nil {
52			return nil, err
53		}
54		return r.GetByID(ctx, id)
55	}
56	
57	func (r *UserRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*User, error) {
58		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users WHERE id = ?`, id)
59		u, err := scanUser(row)
60		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
61			return nil, ErrUserNotFound
62		}
63		return u, err
64	}
65	
66	func (r *UserRepo) GetByUserID(ctx context.Context, userID string) (*User, error) {
67		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users WHERE user_id = ? COLLATE NOCASE`, userID)
68		u, err := scanUser(row)
69		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
70			return nil, ErrUserNotFound
71		}
72		return u, err
73	}
74	
75	func (r *UserRepo) NoteLogin(ctx context.Context, id int64, host string) error {
76		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
77		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
78		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
79			`UPDATE users SET last_login_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, last_host = ?, num_logins = num_logins + 1 WHERE id = ?`,
80			host, id,
81		)
82		return err
83	}
84	
85	func (r *UserRepo) IncrementPosts(ctx context.Context, id int64) error {
86		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
87		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
88		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
89			`UPDATE users SET num_posts = num_posts + 1 WHERE id = ?`, id,
90		)
91		return err
92	}
93

 `./internal/store/boards.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Board struct {
11		ID          int64
12		Name        string
13		Title       string
14		Description string
15		BM          string
16		Attr        int64
17		CreatedAt   time.Time
18	}
19	
20	type BoardRepo struct{ s *Store }
21	
22	var ErrBoardNotFound = errors.New("board not found")
23	
24	const boardColumns = `id, name, title, description, bm, attr, created_at`
25	
26	func scanBoard(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Board, error) {
27		b := &Board{}
28		err := row.Scan(&b.ID, &b.Name, &b.Title, &b.Description, &b.BM, &b.Attr, &b.CreatedAt)
29		return b, err
30	}
31	
32	func (r *BoardRepo) List(ctx context.Context) ([]*Board, error) {
33		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
34			`SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards ORDER BY name COLLATE NOCASE`)
35		if err != nil {
36			return nil, err
37		}
38		defer rows.Close()
39		var out []*Board
40		for rows.Next() {
41			b, err := scanBoard(rows)
42			if err != nil {
43				return nil, err
44			}
45			out = append(out, b)
46		}
47		return out, rows.Err()
48	}
49	
50	func (r *BoardRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Board, error) {
51		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards WHERE id = ?`, id)
52		b, err := scanBoard(row)
53		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
54			return nil, ErrBoardNotFound
55		}
56		return b, err
57	}
58	
59	func (r *BoardRepo) GetByName(ctx context.Context, name string) (*Board, error) {
60		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards WHERE name = ? COLLATE NOCASE`, name)
61		b, err := scanBoard(row)
62		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
63			return nil, ErrBoardNotFound
64		}
65		return b, err
66	}
67	
68	// SeedDefaults is a belt-and-braces complement to migration 0002. It runs
69	// every startup; INSERT OR IGNORE makes it a no-op once the rows exist.
70	func (r *BoardRepo) SeedDefaults(ctx context.Context) error {
71		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
72		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
73		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `
74			INSERT OR IGNORE INTO boards (name, title, description) VALUES
75				('Welcome',  '歡迎來到 SSH-BBS', 'System welcome and announcements'),
76				('Test',     '測試板',           'Try things here. Posts may be cleared periodically.'),
77				('ChitChat', '閒聊',             'General chat. Be kind.')
78		`)
79		return err
80	}
81
```

 `./internal/store/migrations/0001_init.sql`

```text
1	-- Initial schema for the SSH BBS.
2	-- All tables use auto-increment INTEGER PRIMARY KEY plus a unique business
3	-- key where appropriate. We deliberately do NOT mimic pttbbs's recno-based
4	-- addressing (a holdover from mmap'd binary .DIR files) — a relational DB
5	-- removes the recno-tombstone fragility documented in pttbbs.
6	
7	PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
8	
9	CREATE TABLE users (
10	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
11	    user_id         TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- userec_t.userid
12	    password_hash   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- bcrypt; replaces userec_t.passwd (DES)
13	    nickname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.nickname
14	    realname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.realname
15	    email           TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.email
16	    num_logins      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numlogindays (loose)
17	    num_posts       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numposts
18	    last_login_at   DATETIME,                            -- userec_t.lastlogin
19	    last_host       TEXT,                                -- userec_t.lasthost
20	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
21	);
22	
23	CREATE TABLE boards (
24	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
25	    name            TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- boardheader_t.brdname
26	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- boardheader_t.title
27	    description     TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
28	    bm              TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- boardheader_t.BM (comma-sep)
29	    attr            INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- boardheader_t.brdattr (P1+)
30	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
31	);
32	
33	CREATE TABLE articles (
34	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
35	    board_id        INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES boards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
36	    author_id       INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
37	    author_userid   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized (fileheader_t.owner)
38	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- fileheader_t.title
39	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
40	    recommend_score INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- cached sum of pushes (+1) - boos (-1)
41	    filemode        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- fileheader_t.filemode (P1+)
42	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
43	);
44	CREATE INDEX idx_articles_board_created ON articles(board_id, created_at DESC);
45	
46	CREATE TABLE pushes (
47	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
48	    article_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES articles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
49	    user_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
50	    user_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
51	    kind            TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (kind IN ('push','boo','arrow')), -- 推/噓/→
52	    body            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
53	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
54	);
55	CREATE INDEX idx_pushes_article ON pushes(article_id, id);
56	
57	CREATE TABLE water_balloons (
58	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
59	    from_user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
60	    from_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
61	    to_user_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
62	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
63	    delivered_live  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
64	    read_at         DATETIME,
65	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
66	);
67	CREATE INDEX idx_wb_to_unread ON water_balloons(to_user_id, read_at);
68

 `./internal/store/articles.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Article struct {
11		ID              int64
12		BoardID         int64
13		AuthorID        int64
14		AuthorUserID    string
15		Title           string
16		Body            string
17		RecommendScore  int64
18		Filemode        int64
19		CreatedAt       time.Time
20	}
21	
22	type ArticleRepo struct{ s *Store }
23	
24	var ErrArticleNotFound = errors.New("article not found")
25	
26	const articleColumns = `id, board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body,
27		recommend_score, filemode, created_at`
28	
29	func scanArticle(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Article, error) {
30		a := &Article{}
31		err := row.Scan(
32			&a.ID, &a.BoardID, &a.AuthorID, &a.AuthorUserID, &a.Title, &a.Body,
33			&a.RecommendScore, &a.Filemode, &a.CreatedAt,
34		)
35		return a, err
36	}
37	
38	func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoard(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, limit int) ([]*Article, error) {
39		if limit <= 0 {
40			limit = 50
41		}
42		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
43			`SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC LIMIT ?`,
44			boardID, limit,
45		)
46		if err != nil {
47			return nil, err
48		}
49		defer rows.Close()
50		var out []*Article
51		for rows.Next() {
52			a, err := scanArticle(rows)
53			if err != nil {
54				return nil, err
55			}
56			out = append(out, a)
57		}
58		return out, rows.Err()
59	}
60	
61	func (r *ArticleRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Article, error) {
62		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
63		a, err := scanArticle(row)
64		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
65			return nil, ErrArticleNotFound
66		}
67		return a, err
68	}
69	
70	// NeighboursOf returns the article IDs immediately older (prev) and newer
71	// (next) than articleID within the same board, or 0 when there is no
72	// neighbour on that side. "Older" / "newer" are by id (monotonic insert
73	// order); this matches the user-visible "[ / ]" semantics in article view.
74	func (r *ArticleRepo) NeighboursOf(ctx context.Context, boardID, articleID int64) (prev, next int64, err error) {
75		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
76			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id < ? ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1`,
77			boardID, articleID,
78		)
79		if scanErr := row.Scan(&prev); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
80			return 0, 0, scanErr
81		}
82		row = r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
83			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id > ? ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 1`,
84			boardID, articleID,
85		)
86		if scanErr := row.Scan(&next); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
87			return 0, 0, scanErr
88		}
89		return prev, next, nil
90	}
91	
92	func (r *ArticleRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, boardID, authorID int64, authorUserID, title, body string) (*Article, error) {
93		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
94		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
95		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
96			`INSERT INTO articles (board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
97			boardID, authorID, authorUserID, title, body,
98		)
99		if err != nil {
100			return nil, err
101		}
102		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
103		if err != nil {
104			return nil, err
105		}
106		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
107		return scanArticle(row)
108	}
109
```

 `./internal/store/pushes.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	type PushKind string
10	
11	const (
12		PushKindPush  PushKind = "push"  // 推 (+1)
13		PushKindBoo   PushKind = "boo"   // 噓 (-1)
14		PushKindArrow PushKind = "arrow" // → (0)
15	)
16	
17	func (k PushKind) Glyph() string {
18		switch k {
19		case PushKindPush:
20			return "推"
21		case PushKindBoo:
22			return "噓"
23		case PushKindArrow:
24			return "→"
25		}
26		return "?"
27	}
28	
29	type Push struct {
30		ID          int64
31		ArticleID   int64
32		UserID      int64
33		UserUserID  string
34		Kind        PushKind
35		Body        string
36		CreatedAt   time.Time
37	}
38	
39	type PushRepo struct{ s *Store }
40	
41	const pushColumns = `id, article_id, user_id, user_userid, kind, body, created_at`
42	
43	func scanPush(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Push, error) {
44		p := &Push{}
45		var kind string
46		if err := row.Scan(&p.ID, &p.ArticleID, &p.UserID, &p.UserUserID, &kind, &p.Body, &p.CreatedAt); err != nil {
47			return nil, err
48		}
49		p.Kind = PushKind(kind)
50		return p, nil
51	}
52	
53	func (r *PushRepo) ListByArticle(ctx context.Context, articleID int64) ([]*Push, error) {
54		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
55			`SELECT `+pushColumns+` FROM pushes WHERE article_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`, articleID,
56		)
57		if err != nil {
58			return nil, err
59		}
60		defer rows.Close()
61		var out []*Push
62		for rows.Next() {
63			p, err := scanPush(rows)
64			if err != nil {
65				return nil, err
66			}
67			out = append(out, p)
68		}
69		return out, rows.Err()
70	}
71	
72	// Create inserts a push and updates the cached recommend_score on the
73	// article (push: +1, boo: -1, arrow: 0). Both writes happen under the
74	// store-level writeMu so they're effectively atomic from the app's view;
75	// SQLite's WAL gives us read-consistency for the recompute.
76	func (r *PushRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, articleID, userID int64, userUserID string, kind PushKind, body string) (*Push, error) {
77		delta := 0
78		switch kind {
79		case PushKindPush:
80			delta = 1
81		case PushKindBoo:
82			delta = -1
83		case PushKindArrow:
84			delta = 0
85		default:
86			return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid push kind: %q", kind)
87		}
88	
89		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
90		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
91		tx, err := r.s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
92		if err != nil {
93			return nil, err
94		}
95		defer tx.Rollback()
96	
97		res, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
98			`INSERT INTO pushes (article_id, user_id, user_userid, kind, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
99			articleID, userID, userUserID, string(kind), body,
100		)
101		if err != nil {
102			return nil, err
103		}
104		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
105		if err != nil {
106			return nil, err
107		}
108		if delta != 0 {
109			if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
110				`UPDATE articles SET recommend_score = recommend_score + ? WHERE id = ?`,
111				delta, articleID,
112			); err != nil {
113				return nil, err
114			}
115		}
116		if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
117			return nil, err
118		}
119	
120		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+pushColumns+` FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, id)
121		return scanPush(row)
122	}
123
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
9		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
12	)
13	
14	type articleViewModel struct {
15		deps    Deps
16		article *store.Article
17		pushes  []*store.Push
18		width   int
19		height  int
20		scroll  int
21		loadErr error
22	
23		// push input state
24		pushing  bool
25		pushKind store.PushKind
26		pushIn   textinput.Model
27		err      string
28	}
29	
30	func newArticleViewModel(deps Deps, articleID int64) articleViewModel {
31		ctx := context.Background()
32		a, err := deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, articleID)
33		if err != nil {
34			return articleViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
35		}
36		pushes, err := deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, articleID)
37	
38		ti := textinput.New()
39		ti.Placeholder = "comment…"
40		ti.CharLimit = 80
41		ti.Width = 60
42	
43		return articleViewModel{deps: deps, article: a, pushes: pushes, loadErr: err, pushIn: ti}
44	}
45	
46	func (m articleViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
47	
48	func (m articleViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
49		switch msg := msg.(type) {
50		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
51			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
52			return m, nil
53	
54		case PushAddedMsg:
55			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
56				ctx := context.Background()
57				// Re-fetch from DB so timestamps and recommend_score reflect
58				// the canonical state, not the just-synthesized broker payload.
59				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
60					m.pushes = pushes
61				}
62				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
63					m.article = a
64				}
65			}
66			return m, nil
67	
68		case tea.KeyMsg:
69			if m.pushing {
70				return m.updatePushInput(msg)
71			}
72			switch msg.String() {
73			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
74				boardID := int64(0)
75				if m.article != nil {
76					boardID = m.article.BoardID
77				}
78				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: boardID} }
79			case "Q":
80				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
81			case "up", "k":
82				if m.scroll > 0 {
83					m.scroll--
84				}
85			case "down", "j":
86				m.scroll++
87			case "pgup", "b":
88				m.scroll = max(0, m.scroll-10)
89			case "pgdown", " ":
90				m.scroll += 10
91			case "g":
92				m.scroll = 0
93			case "G":
94				m.scroll = max(0, m.bodyLineCount()-m.viewportLines())
95			case "[":
96				if m.article == nil {
97					return m, nil
98				}
99				prev, _, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().NeighboursOf(context.Background(), m.article.BoardID, m.article.ID)
100				if err != nil || prev == 0 {
101					return m, nil
102				}
103				return m, func() tea.Msg {
104					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: prev, BoardID: m.article.BoardID}
105				}
106			case "]":
107				if m.article == nil {
108					return m, nil
109				}
110				_, next, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().NeighboursOf(context.Background(), m.article.BoardID, m.article.ID)
111				if err != nil || next == 0 {
112					return m, nil
113				}
114				return m, func() tea.Msg {
115					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: next, BoardID: m.article.BoardID}
116				}
117			case "+":
118				return m.openPush(store.PushKindPush), nil
119			case "-":
120				return m.openPush(store.PushKindBoo), nil
121			case "=":
122				return m.openPush(store.PushKindArrow), nil
123			}
124		}
125		return m, nil
126	}
127	
128	// bodyLineCount returns the number of lines View will render for the
129	// loaded article (matches strings.Split(body, "\n") used in View).
130	func (m articleViewModel) bodyLineCount() int {
131		if m.article == nil {
132			return 0
133		}
134		return len(strings.Split(m.article.Body, "\n"))
135	}
136	
137	// viewportLines mirrors the maxLines computation in View so g/G/PgDn
138	// agree on how many lines fit on screen.
139	func (m articleViewModel) viewportLines() int {
140		return max(m.height-16, 5)
141	}
142	
143	func (m articleViewModel) openPush(k store.PushKind) tea.Model {
144		m.pushing = true
145		m.pushKind = k
146		m.pushIn.SetValue("")
147		m.pushIn.Focus()
148		m.err = ""
149		return m
150	}
151	
152	func (m articleViewModel) updatePushInput(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
153		switch msg.String() {
154		case "esc":
155			m.pushing = false
156			m.pushIn.Blur()
157			return m, nil
158		case "enter":
159			body := strings.TrimSpace(m.pushIn.Value())
160			if body == "" && m.pushKind != store.PushKindArrow {
161				m.err = "comment required for 推/噓"
162				return m, nil
163			}
164			ctx := context.Background()
165			u := m.deps.User
166			p, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().Create(ctx, m.article.ID, u.ID, u.UserID, m.pushKind, body)
167			if err != nil {
168				m.err = err.Error()
169				return m, nil
170			}
171			// Append locally so the originator sees it immediately.
172			m.pushes = append(m.pushes, p)
173			if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
174				m.article = a
175			}
176			m.pushing = false
177			m.pushIn.Blur()
178			// Broadcast to other live sessions; they filter by ArticleID.
179			if m.deps.Broker != nil {
180				m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, PushAddedMsg{
181					ArticleID:  m.article.ID,
182					UserUserID: u.UserID,
183					Kind:       string(m.pushKind),
184					Body:       body,
185				})
186			}
187			return m, nil
188		}
189		var cmd tea.Cmd
190		m.pushIn, cmd = m.pushIn.Update(msg)
191		return m, cmd
192	}
193	
194	func (m articleViewModel) View() string {
195		if m.loadErr != nil {
196			return "\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n"
197		}
198		if m.article == nil {
199			return "\n  " + StyleDim.Render("(no article)") + "\n"
200		}
201		a := m.article
202	
203		var b strings.Builder
204		b.WriteString("\n")
205		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 文章 #%d · %s ", a.ID, a.AuthorUserID)))
206		b.WriteString("\n\n")
207	
208		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title: ") + a.Title + "\n")
209		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Date:  ") + a.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05") + "\n")
210		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Score: ") + fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore) + "\n")
211		b.WriteString("\n")
212	
213		bodyLines := strings.Split(a.Body, "\n")
214		start := min(m.scroll, len(bodyLines))
215		maxLines := max(m.height-16, 5)
216		end := min(start+maxLines, len(bodyLines))
217		for _, line := range bodyLines[start:end] {
218			b.WriteString("  " + line + "\n")
219		}
220		if start > 0 || end < len(bodyLines) {
221			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("[lines %d-%d / %d]", start+1, end, len(bodyLines))))
222			b.WriteString("\n")
223		}
224	
225		if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
226			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("── 推文 (%d) ──", len(m.pushes))) + "\n")
227			for _, p := range m.pushes {
228				ts := p.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04")
229				line := fmt.Sprintf("  %s %s %s  %s",
230					renderPushKind(p.Kind),
231					PadRight(p.UserUserID, 14),
232					p.Body,
233					StyleDim.Render(ts),
234				)
235				b.WriteString(line + "\n")
236			}
237		}
238	
239		if m.pushing {
240			b.WriteString("\n  ")
241			b.WriteString(renderPushKind(m.pushKind) + " ")
242			b.WriteString(m.pushIn.View())
243			b.WriteString("\n")
244			if m.err != "" {
245				b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
246			}
247			b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("Enter send · Esc cancel"))
248		} else {
249			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("j/k scroll · + 推 · - 噓 · = → · Esc/← back · Ctrl+C disconnect"))
250		}
251		b.WriteString("\n")
252		return b.String()
253	}
254	
255	func renderPushKind(k store.PushKind) string {
256		switch k {
257		case store.PushKindPush:
258			return StylePushKind.Render("推")
259		case store.PushKindBoo:
260			return StyleBooKind.Render("噓")
261		case store.PushKindArrow:
262			return StyleArrowKind.Render("→")
263		}
264		return "?"
265	}
266

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"time"
6	
7		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
8	
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
11	)
12	
13	// Deps bundles everything every screen may need. Pass-by-value into
14	// constructors keeps screens self-contained.
15	type Deps struct {
16		Store      *store.Store
17		Broker     *chat.Broker // nil during register
18		User       *store.User  // nil iff IsRegister
19		IsRegister bool
20	}
21	
22	// Root is the top-level tea.Model. It owns the active sub-model and
23	// handles cross-cutting concerns: window size, global hotkeys, navigation,
24	// and the toast footer.
25	type Root struct {
26		deps   Deps
27		sub    tea.Model
28		width  int
29		height int
30	
31		toast      string
32		toastUntil time.Time
33	}
34	
35	const toastDuration = 3 * time.Second
36	
37	// NewRoot builds the Root with the right initial sub-screen.
38	func NewRoot(deps Deps) Root {
39		r := Root{deps: deps}
40		if deps.IsRegister {
41			r.sub = newRegisterModel(deps)
42		} else {
43			r.sub = newMainMenuModel(deps)
44		}
45		return r
46	}
47	
48	func (m Root) Init() tea.Cmd {
49		return m.sub.Init()
50	}
51	
52	func (m Root) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
53		switch msg := msg.(type) {
54		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
55			m.width = msg.Width
56			m.height = msg.Height
57			var cmd tea.Cmd
58			m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
59			return m, cmd
60	
61		case tea.KeyMsg:
62			// Global ctrl+c always quits.
63			if msg.String() == "ctrl+c" {
64				return m, tea.Quit
65			}
66			// Global Ctrl+U opens water balloon inbox (logged-in only).
67			if msg.String() == "ctrl+u" && !m.deps.IsRegister {
68				return m.navigate(NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox})
69			}
70	
71		case NavigateMsg:
72			return m.navigate(msg)
73	
74		case ErrorMsg:
75			if msg.Err != nil {
76				m.setToast("⚠ " + msg.Err.Error())
77			}
78			return m, nil
79	
80		case WBIncomingMsg:
81			m.setToast("💧 " + msg.FromUserID + ": " + msg.Body)
82			// Mark this WB as read so it doesn't replay on reconnect.
83			if m.deps.Store != nil && msg.ID != 0 {
84				go func(id int64) {
85					_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), id)
86				}(msg.ID)
87			}
88			// fall through so the active screen can also react
89		}
90	
91		var cmd tea.Cmd
92		m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
93		return m, cmd
94	}
95	
96	func (m Root) View() string {
97		v := m.sub.View()
98		if m.toast != "" && time.Now().Before(m.toastUntil) {
99			v += "\n" + StyleToast.Render(m.toast)
100		}
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./internal/store/migrations/0003_add_mail.sql`

```text
1	-- 站內信 (mailbox): persistent threaded private messages.
2	-- Distinct from water_balloons (transient, toast-on-arrival).
3	-- A reply has parent_id pointing to its predecessor and thread_id pointing
4	-- to the root message. For root messages, thread_id = id (filled in by the
5	-- repo's Insert in a follow-up UPDATE inside the same writeMu transaction).
6	
7	CREATE TABLE mail (
8	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
9	    thread_id       INTEGER,                            -- root mail id; back-filled to self for roots
10	    parent_id       INTEGER REFERENCES mail(id),        -- NULL for thread roots
11	    from_user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
12	    from_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                      -- denormalized author handle
13	    to_user_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
14	    subject         TEXT NOT NULL,
15	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
16	    read_at         DATETIME,
17	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
18	);
19	CREATE INDEX idx_mail_to_unread ON mail(to_user_id, read_at);
20	CREATE INDEX idx_mail_thread ON mail(thread_id, id);
21
```
</details></tool-use>

98	func TestArticles_NeighboursOf(t *testing.T) {
99		ctx := context.Background()
100		st := storetest.New(t)
101		user := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
102		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
103		other := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "ChitChat")
104	
105		a, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "first", "")
106		b, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "second", "")
107		c, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "third", "")
108	
109		// Middle article: both neighbours present.
110		prev, next, err := st.Articles().NeighboursOf(ctx, board.ID, b.ID)
111		if err != nil {
112			t.Fatalf("middle: %v", err)
113		}
114		if prev != a.ID || next != c.ID {
115			t.Errorf("middle: prev=%d next=%d, want prev=%d next=%d", prev, next, a.ID, c.ID)
116		}
117	
118		// First article: prev=0, next=second.
119		prev, next, _ = st.Articles().NeighboursOf(ctx, board.ID, a.ID)
120		if prev != 0 || next != b.ID {
121			t.Errorf("first: prev=%d next=%d, want prev=0 next=%d", prev, next, b.ID)
122		}
123	
124		// Last article: prev=second, next=0.
125		prev, next, _ = st.Articles().NeighboursOf(ctx, board.ID, c.ID)
126		if prev != b.ID || next != 0 {
127			t.Errorf("last: prev=%d next=%d, want prev=%d next=0", prev, next, b.ID)
128		}
129	
130		// Wrong board: 0/0 even though the article exists elsewhere.
131		prev, next, _ = st.Articles().NeighboursOf(ctx, other.ID, b.ID)
132		if prev != 0 || next != 0 {
133			t.Errorf("wrong board: prev=%d next=%d, want 0/0", prev, next)
134		}
135	
136		// Single-article board: both 0. Use the empty `other` board for this.
137		d, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, other.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "alone", "")
138		prev, next, _ = st.Articles().NeighboursOf(ctx, other.ID, d.ID)
139		if prev != 0 || next != 0 {
140			t.Errorf("single: prev=%d next=%d, want 0/0", prev, next)
141		}
142	}
143

12	func TestPushes_ScoreDeltas(t *testing.T) {
13		ctx := context.Background()
14		st := storetest.New(t)
15		alice := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
16		bob := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "bob", "")
17		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
18		art, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, alice.ID, alice.UserID, "t", "b")
19	
20		cases := []struct {
21			kind      store.PushKind
22			userID    int64
23			wantScore int64
24		}{
25			{store.PushKindPush, bob.ID, 1},
26			{store.PushKindPush, alice.ID, 2},
27			{store.PushKindBoo, bob.ID, 1},
28			{store.PushKindArrow, alice.ID, 1}, // arrow = 0 delta
29			{store.PushKindBoo, bob.ID, 0},
30			{store.PushKindBoo, bob.ID, -1},
31		}
32		for i, tc := range cases {
33			if _, err := st.Pushes().Create(ctx, art.ID, tc.userID, "x", tc.kind, "msg"); err != nil {
34				t.Fatalf("[%d] Create: %v", i, err)
35			}
36			fresh, err := st.Articles().GetByID(ctx, art.ID)
37			if err != nil {
38				t.Fatalf("[%d] GetByID: %v", i, err)
39			}
40			if fresh.RecommendScore != tc.wantScore {
41				t.Errorf("[%d] kind=%s: score = %d, want %d", i, tc.kind, fresh.RecommendScore, tc.wantScore)
42			}
43		}
44	}
45	
46	func TestPushes_ListPreservesInsertionOrder(t *testing.T) {
47		ctx := context.Background()
48		st := storetest.New(t)
49		alice := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
50		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
51		art, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, alice.ID, alice.UserID, "t", "b")
52	
53		bodies := []string{"first push", "second push", "third push"}
54		for _, body := range bodies {
55			if _, err := st.Pushes().Create(ctx, art.ID, alice.ID, alice.UserID, store.PushKindPush, body); err != nil {
56				t.Fatalf("Create: %v", err)
57			}
58		}

69			}

84	// TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity asserts that N concurrent push inserts
85	// against the same article yield exactly N as the cached score — no lost
86	// updates from interleaved RMW. Run with `go test -race`.
87	func TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity(t *testing.T) {
88		ctx := context.Background()
89		st := storetest.New(t)
90		alice := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
91		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
92		art, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, alice.ID, alice.UserID, "t", "b")
93	
94		const N = 50
95		var wg sync.WaitGroup
96		wg.Add(N)
97		for i := 0; i < N; i++ {
98			go func() {
99				defer wg.Done()
100				if _, err := st.Pushes().Create(ctx, art.ID, alice.ID, alice.UserID, store.PushKindPush, "x"); err != nil {
101					t.Errorf("Create: %v", err)
102				}
103			}()
104		}
105		wg.Wait()
106	
107		fresh, err := st.Articles().GetByID(ctx, art.ID)
108		if err != nil {
109			t.Fatalf("GetByID: %v", err)
110		}
111		if fresh.RecommendScore != int64(N) {
112			t.Errorf("score = %d, want %d (lost update?)", fresh.RecommendScore, N)
113		}
114		pushes, _ := st.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, art.ID)
115		if len(pushes) != N {
116			t.Errorf("inserted %d, found %d in DB", N, len(pushes))
117		}
118	}

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7	
8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
11	)
12	
13	type boardViewModel struct {
14		deps     Deps
15		board    *store.Board
16		articles []*store.Article
17		cursor   int
18		width    int
19		height   int
20		loadErr  error
21	}
22	
23	func newBoardViewModel(deps Deps, boardID int64) boardViewModel {
24		ctx := context.Background()
25		board, err := deps.Store.Boards().GetByID(ctx, boardID)
26		if err != nil {
27			return boardViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
28		}
29		articles, err := deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, 100)
30		return boardViewModel{deps: deps, board: board, articles: articles, loadErr: err}
31	}
32	
33	func (m boardViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
34	
35	func (m boardViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
36		switch msg := msg.(type) {
37		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
38			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
39			return m, nil
40		case ArticleAddedMsg:
41			if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
42				ctx := context.Background()
43				if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, m.board.ID, 100); err == nil {
44					m.articles = arts
45				}
46			}
47			return m, nil
48		case tea.KeyMsg:
49			switch msg.String() {
50			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
51				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardList} }
52			case "Q":
53				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
54			case "up", "k", "[":
55				if m.cursor > 0 {
56					m.cursor--
57				}
58			case "down", "j", "]":
59				if m.cursor < len(m.articles)-1 {
60					m.cursor++
61				}
62			case "home", "g":
63				m.cursor = 0
64			case "end", "G":
65				if len(m.articles) > 0 {
66					m.cursor = len(m.articles) - 1
67				}
68			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
69				if len(m.articles) == 0 {
70					return m, nil
71				}
72				a := m.articles[m.cursor]
73				return m, func() tea.Msg {
74					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: a.ID, BoardID: a.BoardID}
75				}
76			case "p":
77				if m.board == nil {
78					return m, nil
79				}
80				return m, func() tea.Msg {
81					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenPostCompose, BoardID: m.board.ID}
82				}
83			}
84		}
85		return m, nil
86	}
87	
88	func (m boardViewModel) View() string {
89		var b strings.Builder
90		b.WriteString("\n")
91		if m.board != nil {
92			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 看板 %s · %s ", m.board.Name, m.board.Title)))
93		} else {
94			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 看板 "))
95		}
96		b.WriteString("\n\n")
97	
98		if m.loadErr != nil {
99			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
100			return b.String()
```

1	-- Initial schema for the SSH BBS.
2	-- All tables use auto-increment INTEGER PRIMARY KEY plus a unique business
3	-- key where appropriate. We deliberately do NOT mimic pttbbs's recno-based
4	-- addressing (a holdover from mmap'd binary .DIR files) — a relational DB
5	-- removes the recno-tombstone fragility documented in pttbbs.
6	
7	PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
8	
9	CREATE TABLE users (
10	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
11	    user_id         TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- userec_t.userid
12	    password_hash   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- bcrypt; replaces userec_t.passwd (DES)
13	    nickname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.nickname
14	    realname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.realname
15	    email           TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.email
16	    num_logins      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numlogindays (loose)
17	    num_posts       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numposts
18	    last_login_at   DATETIME,                            -- userec_t.lastlogin
19	    last_host       TEXT,                                -- userec_t.lasthost
20	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
21	);
22	
23	CREATE TABLE boards (
24	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
25	    name            TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- boardheader_t.brdname
26	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- boardheader_t.title
27	    description     TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
28	    bm              TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- boardheader_t.BM (comma-sep)
29	    attr            INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- boardheader_t.brdattr (P1+)
30	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
31	);
32	
33	CREATE TABLE articles (
34	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
35	    board_id        INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES boards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
36	    author_id       INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
37	    author_userid   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized (fileheader_t.owner)
38	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- fileheader_t.title
39	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
40	    recommend_score INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- cached sum of pushes (+1) - boos (-1)
41	    filemode        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- fileheader_t.filemode (P1+)
42	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
43	);
44	CREATE INDEX idx_articles_board_created ON articles(board_id, created_at DESC);
45	
46	CREATE TABLE pushes (
47	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
48	    article_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES articles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
49	    user_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
50	    user_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
51	    kind            TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (kind IN ('push','boo','arrow')), -- 推/噓/→
52	    body            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
53	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
54	);
55	CREATE INDEX idx_pushes_article ON pushes(article_id, id);
56	
57	CREATE TABLE water_balloons (
58	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
59	    from_user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
60	    from_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
61	    to_user_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
62	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
63	    delivered_live  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
64	    read_at         DATETIME,
65	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
66	);
67	CREATE INDEX idx_wb_to_unread ON water_balloons(to_user_id, read_at);
68
```

1	-- 站內信 (mailbox): persistent threaded private messages.
2	-- Distinct from water_balloons (transient, toast-on-arrival).
3	-- A reply has parent_id pointing to its predecessor and thread_id pointing
4	-- to the root message. For root messages, thread_id = id (filled in by the
5	-- repo's Insert in a follow-up UPDATE inside the same writeMu transaction).
6	
7	CREATE TABLE mail (
8	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
9	    thread_id       INTEGER,                            -- root mail id; back-filled to self for roots
10	    parent_id       INTEGER REFERENCES mail(id),        -- NULL for thread roots
11	    from_user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
12	    from_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                      -- denormalized author handle
13	    to_user_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
14	    subject         TEXT NOT NULL,
15	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
16	    read_at         DATETIME,
17	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
18	);
19	CREATE INDEX idx_mail_to_unread ON mail(to_user_id, read_at);
20	CREATE INDEX idx_mail_thread ON mail(thread_id, id);
21

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"fmt"
7		"sync"
8	
9		_ "modernc.org/sqlite"
10	)
11	
12	// Store wraps the *sql.DB plus a process-level write mutex used for
13	// multi-statement writes that need to be atomic at the application level
14	// (e.g. inserting a push and recomputing the cached recommend_score).
15	// SQLite WAL handles concurrent reads; this mutex avoids SQLITE_BUSY under
16	// modest contention without spreading sql.Tx everywhere.
17	type Store struct {
18		db      *sql.DB
19		writeMu sync.Mutex
20	
21		users          *UserRepo
22		boards         *BoardRepo
23		articles       *ArticleRepo
24		pushes         *PushRepo
25		waterBalloons  *WaterBalloonRepo
26		mail           *MailRepo
27	}
28	
29	func Open(path string) (*Store, error) {
30		dsn := fmt.Sprintf(
31			"file:%s?_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)&_pragma=foreign_keys(on)&_pragma=busy_timeout(5000)",
32			path,
33		)
34		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dsn)
35		if err != nil {
36			return nil, fmt.Errorf("open sqlite: %w", err)
37		}
38		if err := db.PingContext(context.Background()); err != nil {
39			_ = db.Close()
40			return nil, fmt.Errorf("ping sqlite: %w", err)
41		}
42		if err := apply(context.Background(), db); err != nil {
43			_ = db.Close()
44			return nil, fmt.Errorf("migrate: %w", err)
45		}
46	
47		s := &Store{db: db}
48		s.users = &UserRepo{s: s}
49		s.boards = &BoardRepo{s: s}
50		s.articles = &ArticleRepo{s: s}
51		s.pushes = &PushRepo{s: s}
52		s.waterBalloons = &WaterBalloonRepo{s: s}
53		s.mail = &MailRepo{s: s}
54		return s, nil
55	}
56	
57	func (s *Store) Close() error { return s.db.Close() }
58	
59	// DB exposes the raw handle for callers that need it (tests, ad-hoc SQL).
60	// Production code should go through repos.
61	func (s *Store) DB() *sql.DB { return s.db }
62	
63	func (s *Store) Users() *UserRepo                 { return s.users }
64	func (s *Store) Boards() *BoardRepo               { return s.boards }
65	func (s *Store) Articles() *ArticleRepo           { return s.articles }
66	func (s *Store) Pushes() *PushRepo                { return s.pushes }
67	func (s *Store) WaterBalloons() *WaterBalloonRepo { return s.waterBalloons }
68	func (s *Store) Mail() *MailRepo                  { return s.mail }
69
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# Pitfalls
2	
3	Past traps we've stepped on. **Symptoms-first** knowledge base — when a
4	problem recurs (on a new machine, after an upgrade, with a new tool combo),
5	grepping the symptom here lands you on the root cause and workaround in
6	seconds, instead of re-debugging from scratch.
7	
8	This folder is excluded from <DEPLOYMENT/PACKAGING MECHANISM, e.g. chezmoi via
9	.chezmoiignore.tmpl, Python via MANIFEST.in, npm via .npmignore> — it is repo
10	metadata for maintainers, not user-facing config to deploy.
11	
12	## Pitfalls vs the rest
13	
14	| Surface | Time direction | Question it answers | Access pattern |
15	|---|---|---|---|
16	| `docs/<tool>.md` | Present | "How does this tool work / how do I configure it?" | Read top to bottom |
17	| `pitfalls/<slug>.md` | **Past** | **"I see error X — has this happened before?"** | **Grep symptoms** |
18	| `backlog/<slug>.md` | Future | "We thought about doing Y — what was the analysis?" | Index in `TODO.md` |
19	| `AGENTS.md` Hard invariants | Present | "What rules MUST agents follow?" | Read top to bottom |
20	
21	A pitfall **graduates** to a Hard invariant when the trap is serious enough
22	that you can't rely on memory or grep — typically when (a) it recurs across
23	machines, (b) it silently corrupts state, or (c) the workaround is non-obvious
24	and easy to undo by accident. When graduating, leave a `pitfalls/<slug>.md`
25	as historical record and link to it from the new invariant.
26	
27	## When to add a pitfall doc
28	
29	Add `pitfalls/<slug>.md` when you've spent more than ~15 minutes on something
30	that wasn't googleable, AND any of:
31	
32	- The symptom is non-obvious from the root cause (silent state, weird side
33	  effect, behaviour change without error)
34	- The fix is "do nothing different but in a specific order"
35	- The same trap could be hit by a new agent / new machine / new contributor
36	- An upstream bug exists with no ETA — workaround needs to outlive memory
37	- A specific tool version is required (or forbidden) and failure at the
38	  wrong version is silent / confusing
39	
40	## When NOT to add a pitfall doc
41	
42	- Trivially googleable (next person solves in 30 seconds)
43	- Already covered in `docs/<tool>.md` — cross-link from this README's
44	  "Cross-referenced pitfalls" table below instead of duplicating
45	- Already a Hard invariant (cross-link only)
46	- One-off transient (network glitch, machine-specific config rot)
47	
48	## File template
49	
50	See [`pitfall-doc.md.template`] in the
51	[`project-knowledge-harness` skill](https://github.com/daviddwlee84/agent-skills/tree/main/skills/local/project-knowledge-harness)
52	for the per-doc template.
53	
54	Key sections (different from `backlog/` template — symptom-first, not
55	context-first):
56	
57	```markdown
58	# <Title describing the SYMPTOM, not the root cause>
59	
60	**Symptoms** (grep this section): <verbatim error messages, observable behaviour>
61	**First seen**: YYYY-MM
62	**Affects**: <tool/version/OS combo>
63	**Status**: workaround documented / fixed upstream in vX.Y / WONTFIX
64	
65	## Symptom
66	
67	Full error messages (verbatim — preserves grep-ability).
68	
69	## Root cause
70	
71	Why this happens, with source/docs/upstream issue link.
72	
73	## Workaround
74	
75	Copy-pasteable commands or config diff.
76	
77	## Prevention
78	
79	How to avoid stepping on this again.
80	
81	## Related
82	
83	Links to docs, sibling pitfalls, TODO entries, upstream issues.
84	```
85	
86	## Index
87	
88	Pitfalls owned by this folder. Keep alphabetical.
89	
90	| Slug | Symptom keywords | Status |
91	|---|---|---|
92	| (none yet) | | |
93	
94	## Cross-referenced pitfalls (still in their original homes)
95	
96	These traps are documented elsewhere and aren't duplicated here — the table
97	exists so grepping `pitfalls/` still finds them. Move into this folder only
98	if their original location stops being a natural reading flow.
99	
100	| Trap | Lives in | Why not here |
101	|---|---|---|
102	| (example: Tool X version Y bug) | `docs/tool-x.md` → "Known issues" | Already part of the tool's normal config narrative |
103
```
</details></tool-use>

Excellent. Let me now compile my comprehensive findings report:

---

## Authentication & User Model Report

### 1. User Schema

**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/0001_init.sql` (lines 9–21)

**`users` table columns:**
- `id` — INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT
- `user_id` — TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE (business key; 3–12 chars)
- `password_hash` — TEXT NOT NULL (bcrypt; replaces legacy DES)
- `nickname` — TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '' (up to 32 Unicode chars)
- `realname` — TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''
- `email` — TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '' (max 128 chars)
- `num_logins` — INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
- `num_posts` — INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
- `last_login_at` — DATETIME (nullable)
- `last_host` — TEXT (nullable; client IP)
- `created_at` — DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

**Go model:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/users.go` (lines 10–22)
- Maps exactly to schema; uses `sql.NullTime` and `sql.NullString` for nullable fields

**Status:** No role, permission, admin, or ban fields exist. `bm` field exists in **`boards`** table (line 15 of boards.go) as comma-separated board manager list (unused, legacy pttbbs compatibility).

---

### 2. Auth Package

**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/auth/auth.go`

**Exported constants:**
- `ReservedUsernameNew = "new"` (line 17) — SSH username sentinel triggering in-TUI registration
- No other exported constants related to roles/permissions

**Validation constants (internal, lines 19–25):**
- `bcryptCost = 12` (line 20)
- `minPasswordLen = 6`, `maxPasswordLen = 128`
- `maxNickname = 32`, `maxEmail = 128`
- `userIDRe = ^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_]{2,11}$` (line 37)

**Function signatures:**
- `Register(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, userID, password, nickname, email string) (*store.User, error)` (line 41)
  - Validates inputs, hashes with `bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(password), bcryptCost)` (line 62)
  - Returns `ErrUserExists`, `ErrInvalidUserID`, `ErrInvalidPassword`, `ErrNicknameTooLong`, `ErrEmailTooLong`, `ErrReservedUsername`, or success

- `VerifyLogin(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, userID, password, host string) (*store.User, error)` (line 80)
  - Fetches user by `user_id`, compares hash with `bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword()` (line 90)
  - **Timing-safe:** dummy bcrypt check on user-not-found (line 85)
  - Calls `st.Users().NoteLogin(ctx, u.ID, host)` on success (line 93), updates `last_login_at`, `last_host`, and increments `num_logins`
  - Returns `ErrBadCredentials` on mismatch

**Status:** No `MustChangePassword` flag, no role/permission functions, no "first-login" concept. Plain bcrypt cost 12 (≈500ms per check).

---

### 3. Password-Auth Callback & SSH Context Plumbing

**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/server/auth_middleware.go`

**Context keys (lines 14–20):**
```go
const (
    ctxKeyUserID ctxKey = "user_id"      // int64; set on real login
    ctxKeyRegister ctxKey = "register"   // bool; true if SSH user == "new"
    ctxKeyRemoteHost ctxKey = "remote_host" // string; client IP
)
```

**`PasswordAuth` callback (lines 22–43):**
- Detects `ctx.User() == auth.ReservedUsernameNew` → sets `ctxKeyRegister = true` and returns `true` (allows entry) (lines 30–32)
- Otherwise calls `auth.VerifyLogin(context.Background(), st, ctx.User(), password, host)` (line 34)
- On success: sets `ctxKeyUserID = user.ID` (line 39)
- On failure: logs and returns `false` (line 37)
- Always extracts `RemoteAddr()` and stashes via `ctx.SetValue(ctxKeyRemoteHost, ...)` (lines 25–28)

**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/server/server.go`

**`makeProgramHandler` routing (lines 41–96):**
- Checks `ctx.Value(ctxKeyRegister).(bool)` — if true, creates `newRegisterModel(deps)` with `IsRegister: true` (lines 46–49)
  - No broker registration during register-mode
- Otherwise fetches user by `user_id` from context (line 52) and creates `newMainMenuModel(deps)` with full `User` pointer (lines 57–64)
- Registers session into broker for live water-balloon delivery (line 69)
- Drains unread water balloons from DB and replays as toasts (lines 75–85)

**Fallthrough:** If no `user_id` in context (neither register mode nor real login), session is rejected (lines 53–56).

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/ptt_trace_code/01_userec.md`

```text
1	# 01 · `userec_t` → `users` table
2	
3	## pttbbs reference
4	
5	`include/pttstruct.h` defines `userec_t`, ~500 bytes per user, persisted as a
6	fixed-record array in `.PASSWDS`. Lookups are by hash on `userid`. The struct
7	includes account credentials, vanity fields (real name, nickname, email),
8	counters (logins, posts, money), game stats (five-in-a-row, chess, go),
9	last-login metadata, and a flags bitmap.
10	
11	```c
12	typedef struct userec_t {
13	    char    userid[IDLEN+1];     // 13
14	    char    realname[20];
15	    char    nickname[24];
16	    char    passwd[14];          // crypt(3) DES
17	    int32_t uflag, userlevel;
18	    uint16_t numlogindays, numposts;
19	    char    lasthost[16];
20	    int32_t lastlogin;
21	    // ... ~30 more fields
22	} userec_t;
23	```
24	
25	## Our mapping (`internal/store/users.go` + `migrations/0001_init.sql`)
26	
27	```sql
28	CREATE TABLE users (
29	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
30	    user_id         TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- userec_t.userid
31	    password_hash   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- bcrypt cost 12 (was DES)
32	    nickname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
33	    realname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
34	    email           TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
35	    num_logins      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- ~userec_t.numlogindays
36	    num_posts       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
37	    last_login_at   DATETIME,
38	    last_host       TEXT,
39	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
40	);
41	```
42	
43	## What changed and why
44	
45	- **`id` (auto-inc) is the canonical key**, not `user_id`. PTT uses the
46	  `user_id` string everywhere because its hash table indexes on it; we get
47	  fast lookups via `UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE` so the int id is purely internal.
48	- **No `userlevel` / `uflag` bitmaps yet.** PTT's permission model is a
49	  bag-of-bits accreted over 20 years. Adding it on demand (P1+) is cheaper
50	  than reverse-engineering the existing bits.
51	- **No money / game counters.** Out of scope for v1.
52	- **`COLLATE NOCASE`** so `ALICE`, `Alice`, `alice` collide on UNIQUE and
53	  resolve to the same login — matches pttbbs `strcasecmp` lookups.
54	- **`bcrypt` not `crypt(3)`.** `crypt(3)` DES truncates to 8 chars and is
55	  trivially brute-forced. `auth.bcryptCost = 12`.
56	
57	## Reserved username
58	
59	`new` is reserved (`auth.ReservedUsernameNew`). The auth middleware
60	(`internal/server/auth_middleware.go`) intercepts SSH user `new` and routes
61	to the in-TUI register form. `auth.Register` rejects `new` to keep the
62	sentinel from ever colliding with a real account.
63
```
</details></tool-use>

### 3. Schema: `articles` and `pushes` Tables
**From `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/0001_init.sql`:**

**articles** (lines 33–43):
```sql
CREATE TABLE articles (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    board_id        INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES boards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    author_id       INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    author_userid   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
    title           TEXT NOT NULL,
    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
    recommend_score INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    filemode        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,

```
— **No deleted_at, deleted_by, or soft_delete column.** Only hard-delete via FK cascade is possible today.

**pushes** (lines 46–55):
```sql
CREATE TABLE pushes (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    article_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES articles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    user_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    user_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
    kind            TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (kind IN ('push','boo','arrow')),
    body            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',

);
```
— **No deleted_at, deleted_by, or soft_delete column.**

1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"flag"
7		"log"
8		"os"
9		"os/signal"
10		"syscall"
11		"time"
12	
13		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
14		"github.com/charmbracelet/ssh"
15	
16		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
17		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/server"
18		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
19		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/version"
20	)
21	
22	func main() {
23		addr := flag.String("addr", ":2222", "SSH listen address")
24		dbPath := flag.String("db", "data/bbs.db", "SQLite database path")
25		hostkey := flag.String("hostkey", ".ssh/host_ed25519", "SSH host key path")
26		flag.Parse()
27	
28		st, err := store.Open(*dbPath)
29		if err != nil {
30			log.Fatalf("store.Open: %v", err)
31		}
32		defer st.Close()
33		if err := st.Boards().SeedDefaults(context.Background()); err != nil {
34			log.Fatalf("seed boards: %v", err)
35		}
36		log.Printf("storage ready at %s", *dbPath)
37	
38		broker := chat.NewBroker()
39	
40		srv, err := server.New(server.Config{Addr: *addr, HostKey: *hostkey}, st, broker)
41		if err != nil {
42			log.Fatalf("server.New: %v", err)
43		}
44	
45		ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
46		defer stop()
47	
48		errCh := make(chan error, 1)
49		go func() {
50			log.Printf("sshbbs %s listening on %s", version.Version, *addr)
51			errCh <- srv.ListenAndServe()
52		}()
53	
54		select {
55		case err := <-errCh:
56			if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, ssh.ErrServerClosed) {
57				log.Fatalf("listen: %v", err)
58			}
59		case <-ctx.Done():
60			log.Println("shutdown signal received")
61			shutdown(srv, broker)
62		}
63	}
64	
65	// shutdown closes the listener (refuses new connections), then asks every
66	// live bubbletea program to quit, then closes the SSH server hard. The DB
67	// closes on the deferred st.Close() in main.
68	func shutdown(srv *ssh.Server, broker *chat.Broker) {
69		const drainTimeout = 3 * time.Second
70	
71		// 1. Stop accepting new connections.
72		listenerCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), drainTimeout)
73		defer cancel()
74		if err := srv.Shutdown(listenerCtx); err != nil {
75			log.Printf("server.Shutdown: %v", err)
76		}
77	
78		// 2. Tell every active session to quit.
79		sessions := broker.SessionsSnapshot()
80		if len(sessions) == 0 {
81			return
82		}
83		log.Printf("draining %d active session(s)", len(sessions))
84		for _, s := range sessions {
85			s.Program.Send(tea.Quit())
86		}
87	
88		// 3. Give them a moment to flush their final view, then force-close.
89		timer := time.NewTimer(drainTimeout)
90		defer timer.Stop()
91		for {
92			if len(broker.SessionsSnapshot()) == 0 {
93				log.Println("all sessions drained")
94				return
95			}
96			select {
97			case <-timer.C:
98				log.Printf("drain timeout; %d session(s) still attached", len(broker.SessionsSnapshot()))
99				return
100			case <-time.After(100 * time.Millisecond):
101			}
102		}
103	}
104

131		for i := 0; i+len(sub) <= len(s); i++ {
132			if s[i:i+len(sub)] == sub {

134			}
135		}

1	package server
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5	
6		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
7		"github.com/charmbracelet/log"
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/ssh"
9		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish"
10		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/activeterm"
11		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/bubbletea"
12		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/logging"
13		"github.com/muesli/termenv"
14	
15		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
16		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
17		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/tui"
18	)
19	
20	type Config struct {
21		Addr    string
22		HostKey string
23	}
24	
25	func New(cfg Config, st *store.Store, broker *chat.Broker) (*ssh.Server, error) {
26		return wish.NewServer(
27			wish.WithAddress(cfg.Addr),
28			wish.WithHostKeyPath(cfg.HostKey),
29			wish.WithPasswordAuth(PasswordAuth(st)),
30			wish.WithMiddleware(
31				bubbletea.MiddlewareWithProgramHandler(makeProgramHandler(st, broker), termenv.ANSI256),
32				activeterm.Middleware(),
33				logging.Middleware(),
34			),
35		)
36	}
37	
38	// makeProgramHandler is the wish ProgramHandler. It owns *tea.Program
39	// creation so we can hand the pointer to the chat broker for live message
40	// delivery, and arrange Unregister when the SSH session ends.
41	func makeProgramHandler(st *store.Store, broker *chat.Broker) bubbletea.ProgramHandler {
42		return func(sess ssh.Session) *tea.Program {
43			ctx := sess.Context()
44	
45			// Register-mode: SSH user was "new". No DB user, no broker registration.
46			if reg, ok := ctx.Value(ctxKeyRegister).(bool); ok && reg {
47				deps := tui.Deps{Store: st, Broker: broker, IsRegister: true}
48				m := tui.NewRoot(deps)
49				return tea.NewProgram(m, append(bubbletea.MakeOptions(sess), tea.WithAltScreen())...)
50			}
51	
52			uid, _ := ctx.Value(ctxKeyUserID).(int64)
53			if uid == 0 {
54				log.Warn("session has no user_id; rejecting", "remote", sess.RemoteAddr())
55				return nil
56			}
57			user, err := st.Users().GetByID(context.Background(), uid)
58			if err != nil {
59				log.Error("load user", "id", uid, "err", err)
60				return nil
61			}
62	
63			deps := tui.Deps{Store: st, User: user, Broker: broker}
64			m := tui.NewRoot(deps)
65			p := tea.NewProgram(m, append(bubbletea.MakeOptions(sess), tea.WithAltScreen())...)
66	
67			// Hook session into the broker so other users can send to us.
68			cs := &chat.Session{UserID: user.ID, UserIDStr: user.UserID, Program: p}
69			broker.Register(cs)
70			log.Info("session registered", "user", user.UserID, "online", len(broker.OnlineList()))
71	
72			// Drain unread water balloons from DB and replay them as toasts.
73			// We do this *before* the program starts so the messages land in
74			// the model's first Update(); tea.Program.Send buffers if needed.
75			go func() {
76				unread, err := st.WaterBalloons().ListUnreadFor(context.Background(), user.ID)
77				if err != nil {
78					log.Warn("drain unread WBs", "user", user.UserID, "err", err)
79					return
80				}
81				for _, w := range unread {
82					p.Send(tui.WBIncomingMsg{ID: w.ID, FromUserID: w.FromUserIDStr, Body: w.Body})
83					_ = st.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), w.ID)
84				}
85			}()
86	
87			// On session end, unregister so we stop trying to deliver to a dead Program.
88			go func() {
89				<-ctx.Done()
90				broker.Unregister(user.ID, p)
91				log.Info("session unregistered", "user", user.UserID, "online", len(broker.OnlineList()))
92			}()
93	
94			return p
95		}
96	}
97
```
