```go
package tui

import (
	"context"
	"errors"
	"strings"

	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/auth"
)

// Form-style screen routed from Root when deps.MustChangePassword is set.
// Like screen_register, it deliberately does NOT bind h/l so those keys
// remain available for text editing inside the password fields.

const (
	pwFieldCurrent = iota
	pwFieldNew
	pwFieldConfirm
	pwFieldCount
)

type passwordChangeModel struct {
	deps    Deps
	inputs  []textinput.Model
	focus   int
	err     string
	success bool
}

func newPasswordChangeModel(deps Deps) passwordChangeModel {
	inputs := make([]textinput.Model, pwFieldCount)
	for i := range inputs {
		ti := textinput.New()
		ti.EchoMode = textinput.EchoPassword
		ti.EchoCharacter = '•'
		ti.CharLimit = 128
		ti.Width = 32
		inputs[i] = ti
	}
	inputs[pwFieldCurrent].Placeholder = "目前密碼"
	inputs[pwFieldNew].Placeholder = "新密碼 (≥ 6)"
	inputs[pwFieldConfirm].Placeholder = "再次輸入新密碼"
	inputs[pwFieldCurrent].Focus()
	return passwordChangeModel{deps: deps, inputs: inputs}
}

func (m passwordChangeModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }

func (m passwordChangeModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
	switch msg := msg.(type) {
	case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
		return m, nil

	case tea.KeyMsg:
		if m.success {
			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
		}
		switch msg.String() {
		case "esc":
			// Refusing to set a new password is allowed but ends the session;
			// the must_change flag is still set, so reconnecting just lands
			// back on this screen. Cleaner than letting the user limbo on a
			// screen they refused.
			return m, tea.Quit
		case "tab", "down":
			m.advance(+1)
			return m, nil
		case "shift+tab", "up":
			m.advance(-1)
			return m, nil
		case "enter":
			if m.focus < len(m.inputs)-1 {
				m.advance(+1)
				return m, nil
			}
			return m.submit()
		}
	}

	var cmd tea.Cmd
	m.inputs[m.focus], cmd = m.inputs[m.focus].Update(msg)
	return m, cmd
}

func (m *passwordChangeModel) advance(d int) {
	n := len(m.inputs)
	m.focus = (m.focus + d + n) % n
	for i := range m.inputs {
		if i == m.focus {
			m.inputs[i].Focus()
		} else {
			m.inputs[i].Blur()
		}
	}
}

func (m passwordChangeModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
	current := m.inputs[pwFieldCurrent].Value()
	newPw := m.inputs[pwFieldNew].Value()
	confirm := m.inputs[pwFieldConfirm].Value()

	if m.deps.User == nil {
		m.err = "internal error: no user"
		return m, nil
	}
	if err := auth.VerifyPasswordHash(m.deps.User.PasswordHash, current); err != nil {
		m.err = "目前密碼錯誤"
		return m, nil
	}
	if err := auth.ValidatePassword(newPw); err != nil {
		m.err = err.Error()
		return m, nil
	}
	if newPw != confirm {
		m.err = "新密碼與確認不一致"
		return m, nil
	}
	if newPw == current {
		m.err = "新密碼不可與目前密碼相同"
		return m, nil
	}

	hash, err := auth.HashPassword(newPw)
	if err != nil {
		m.err = err.Error()
		return m, nil
	}
	ctx := context.Background()
	if err := m.deps.Store.Users().SetPassword(ctx, m.deps.User.ID, hash); err != nil {
		m.err = err.Error()
		return m, nil
	}
	// Refresh User in place so subsequent screens see the cleared flag.
	fresh, err := m.deps.Store.Users().GetByID(ctx, m.deps.User.ID)
	if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, errUnused()) {
		m.err = err.Error()
		return m, nil
	}
	if fresh != nil {
		*m.deps.User = *fresh
	}
	m.success = true
	return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
}

// errUnused is a nil sentinel for errors.Is — lets the assignment compile
// without importing a sentinel error we don't actually need to match.
func errUnused() error { return nil }

func (m passwordChangeModel) View() string {
	var b strings.Builder
	b.WriteString("\n")
	b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render("  === 修改密碼 (首次登入必須修改) ==="))
	b.WriteString("\n\n")

	labels := []string{"目前密碼 current", "新密碼 new (≥ 6)", "再次輸入 confirm"}
	for i, label := range labels {
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(label))
		b.WriteString("\n  ")
		b.WriteString(m.inputs[i].View())
		b.WriteString("\n\n")
	}

	if m.err != "" {
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n\n")
	}
	if m.success {
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleSuccess.Render("✓ 已更新，將進入主選單…") + "\n\n")
	}
	b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab/↓ next · Shift+Tab/↑ prev · Enter submit · Esc disconnect"))
	b.WriteString("\n")
	return b.String()
}

```

```text
11	// keyOf builds a tea.KeyMsg whose .String() matches the wanted form.
12	// bubbletea exposes typed key constants; we use Runes for ASCII letters
13	// and named Type values for special keys.
14	func keyOf(s string) tea.KeyMsg {
15		switch s {
16		case "enter":
17			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyEnter}
18		case "esc":
19			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyEsc}
20		case "left":
21			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyLeft}
22		case "right":
23			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyRight}
24		case "up":
25			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyUp}
26		case "down":
27			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyDown}
28		case "backspace":
29			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyBackspace}
30		case "ctrl+u":
31			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyCtrlU}
32		case "ctrl+c":
33			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyCtrlC}
34		case " ":
35			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeySpace}
36		}
37		return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyRunes, Runes: []rune(s)}
38	}
39	
40	// runCmd invokes the tea.Cmd returned by Update and returns the resulting
41	// tea.Msg (or nil if cmd is nil).
42	func runCmd(cmd tea.Cmd) tea.Msg {
43		if cmd == nil {
44			return nil
45		}
46		return cmd()
47	}

74	func TestMainMenu_CursorClamps(t *testing.T) {
```

```go
package tui

import (
	"context"
	"testing"

	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/auth"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store/storetest"
)

// Build a fresh store, seed `admin` with a known password and
// must_change_password=1, return a password-change model bound to it.
func newPasswordChangeFixture(t *testing.T, current string) (passwordChangeModel, *store.Store, *store.User) {
	t.Helper()
	st := storetest.New(t)
	ctx := context.Background()
	hash, err := auth.HashPassword(current)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("HashPassword: %v", err)
	}
	u, err := st.Users().InsertSystemAccount(ctx, "admin", hash, store.RoleAdmin, true)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("InsertSystemAccount: %v", err)
	}
	deps := Deps{Store: st, User: u, MustChangePassword: true}
	m := newPasswordChangeModel(deps)
	return m, st, u
}

func setPwField(m passwordChangeModel, idx int, value string) passwordChangeModel {
	m.inputs[idx].SetValue(value)
	return m
}

func TestPasswordChange_RejectsWrongCurrent(t *testing.T) {
	m, _, _ := newPasswordChangeFixture(t, "old-password")
	m = setPwField(m, pwFieldCurrent, "totally-wrong")
	m = setPwField(m, pwFieldNew, "new-pw-1")
	m = setPwField(m, pwFieldConfirm, "new-pw-1")

	model, _ := m.submit()
	got := model.(passwordChangeModel)
	if got.success {
		t.Error("submit succeeded with wrong current password")
	}
	if got.err == "" {
		t.Error("expected error message; got empty")
	}
}

func TestPasswordChange_RejectsConfirmMismatch(t *testing.T) {
	m, _, _ := newPasswordChangeFixture(t, "old-password")
	m = setPwField(m, pwFieldCurrent, "old-password")
	m = setPwField(m, pwFieldNew, "new-pw-1")
	m = setPwField(m, pwFieldConfirm, "different")

	model, _ := m.submit()
	got := model.(passwordChangeModel)
	if got.success {
		t.Error("submit succeeded with confirm mismatch")
	}
}

func TestPasswordChange_RejectsTooShort(t *testing.T) {
	m, _, _ := newPasswordChangeFixture(t, "old-password")
	m = setPwField(m, pwFieldCurrent, "old-password")
	m = setPwField(m, pwFieldNew, "x")
	m = setPwField(m, pwFieldConfirm, "x")

	model, _ := m.submit()
	got := model.(passwordChangeModel)
	if got.success {
		t.Error("submit succeeded with too-short new password")
	}
}

func TestPasswordChange_RejectsSameAsCurrent(t *testing.T) {
	m, _, _ := newPasswordChangeFixture(t, "old-pw-12")
	m = setPwField(m, pwFieldCurrent, "old-pw-12")
	m = setPwField(m, pwFieldNew, "old-pw-12")
	m = setPwField(m, pwFieldConfirm, "old-pw-12")

	model, _ := m.submit()
	got := model.(passwordChangeModel)
	if got.success {
		t.Error("submit succeeded with new == current")
	}
}

func TestPasswordChange_Success_ClearsFlagAndNavigates(t *testing.T) {
	m, st, u := newPasswordChangeFixture(t, "old-password")
	m = setPwField(m, pwFieldCurrent, "old-password")
	m = setPwField(m, pwFieldNew, "fresh-pw-9")
	m = setPwField(m, pwFieldConfirm, "fresh-pw-9")

	model, cmd := m.submit()
	got := model.(passwordChangeModel)
	if !got.success {
		t.Fatalf("submit failed: %s", got.err)
	}
	msg := runCmd(cmd)
	nav, ok := msg.(NavigateMsg)
	if !ok {
		t.Fatalf("got %T, want NavigateMsg", msg)
	}
	if nav.To != ScreenMainMenu {
		t.Errorf("To = %v, want ScreenMainMenu", nav.To)
	}

	// Verify flag is cleared in DB and the in-memory User pointer is refreshed.
	fresh, err := st.Users().GetByID(context.Background(), u.ID)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("re-fetch: %v", err)
	}
	if fresh.MustChangePassword {
		t.Error("must_change_password not cleared in DB")
	}
	if u.MustChangePassword {
		t.Error("in-memory User.MustChangePassword not cleared (Deps.User pointer not refreshed)")
	}
	// And the new password must verify.
	if err := auth.VerifyPasswordHash(fresh.PasswordHash, "fresh-pw-9"); err != nil {
		t.Errorf("new password does not verify against stored hash: %v", err)
	}
}

func TestPasswordChange_EscQuits(t *testing.T) {
	m, _, _ := newPasswordChangeFixture(t, "old-password")
	_, cmd := m.Update(keyOf("esc"))
	msg := runCmd(cmd)
	if _, ok := msg.(tea.QuitMsg); !ok {
		t.Errorf("got %T, want tea.QuitMsg", msg)
	}
}
```

```text
13	func TestArticles_CreateAndGet(t *testing.T) {

17		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")

24			t.Error("Create returned zero ID")
25		}
26		if got.Title != "標題" || got.Body != "內容\n第二行" {
27			t.Errorf("title/body mismatch: %q / %q", got.Title, got.Body)
28		}
29		if got.RecommendScore != 0 {
30			t.Errorf("RecommendScore = %d, want 0", got.RecommendScore)
31		}

34		if err != nil {
35			t.Fatalf("GetByID: %v", err)
36		}
37		if fetched.AuthorUserID != "alice" {
38			t.Errorf("AuthorUserID = %q, want alice", fetched.AuthorUserID)
39		}

42	func TestArticles_GetByID_NotFound(t *testing.T) {
43		st := storetest.New(t)
44		_, err := st.Articles().GetByID(context.Background(), 9999)
45		if !errors.Is(err, store.ErrArticleNotFound) {
46			t.Errorf("got %v, want ErrArticleNotFound", err)
47		}

50	func TestArticles_ListByBoard_NewestFirst(t *testing.T) {
51		ctx := context.Background()
52		st := storetest.New(t)
53		user := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
54		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
55	
56		// Insert three with explicit small sleeps so created_at differs.
57		for i, title := range []string{"first", "second", "third"} {
58			if _, err := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, title, "body"); err != nil {
59				t.Fatalf("create %d: %v", i, err)
60			}
61			time.Sleep(15 * time.Millisecond)
62		}
63	
64		got, err := st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, board.ID, 0)
65		if err != nil {
66			t.Fatalf("ListByBoard: %v", err)
67		}
68		if len(got) != 3 {
69			t.Fatalf("got %d, want 3", len(got))
70		}
71		// Newest-first.
72		wantOrder := []string{"third", "second", "first"}
73		for i, w := range wantOrder {
74			if got[i].Title != w {
75				t.Errorf("[%d] = %q, want %q", i, got[i].Title, w)
76			}
77		}
78	}
79	
80	func TestArticles_ListByBoard_Limit(t *testing.T) {
81		ctx := context.Background()
82		st := storetest.New(t)
83		user := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
84		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
85	
86		for i := 0; i < 5; i++ {
87			_, _ = st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "t", "b")
88		}
89		got, err := st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, board.ID, 2)
90		if err != nil {
91			t.Fatalf("ListByBoard: %v", err)
92		}
93		if len(got) != 2 {
94			t.Errorf("got %d, want 2", len(got))
95		}
96	}
97	
98	func TestArticles_NeighboursOf(t *testing.T) {
99		ctx := context.Background()
100		st := storetest.New(t)
101		user := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
102		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
103		other := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "ChitChat")
104	
105		a, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "first", "")
106		b, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "second", "")
107		c, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "third", "")
108	
109		// Middle article: both neighbours present.
110		prev, next, err := st.Articles().NeighboursOf(ctx, board.ID, b.ID)
111		if err != nil {
112			t.Fatalf("middle: %v", err)
113		}
114		if prev != a.ID || next != c.ID {
115			t.Errorf("middle: prev=%d next=%d, want prev=%d next=%d", prev, next, a.ID, c.ID)
116		}
117	
118		// First article: prev=0, next=second.
119		prev, next, _ = st.Articles().NeighboursOf(ctx, board.ID, a.ID)
120		if prev != 0 || next != b.ID {
121			t.Errorf("first: prev=%d next=%d, want prev=0 next=%d", prev, next, b.ID)
122		}
123	
124		// Last article: prev=second, next=0.
125		prev, next, _ = st.Articles().NeighboursOf(ctx, board.ID, c.ID)
126		if prev != b.ID || next != 0 {
127			t.Errorf("last: prev=%d next=%d, want prev=%d next=0", prev, next, b.ID)
128		}
129	
130		// Wrong board: 0/0 even though the article exists elsewhere.
131		prev, next, _ = st.Articles().NeighboursOf(ctx, other.ID, b.ID)
132		if prev != 0 || next != 0 {
133			t.Errorf("wrong board: prev=%d next=%d, want 0/0", prev, next)
134		}
135	
136		// Single-article board: both 0. Use the empty `other` board for this.
137		d, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, other.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "alone", "")
138		prev, next, _ = st.Articles().NeighboursOf(ctx, other.ID, d.ID)
139		if prev != 0 || next != 0 {
140			t.Errorf("single: prev=%d next=%d, want 0/0", prev, next)
141		}
142	}
143	
144	func TestArticles_BoardIsolation(t *testing.T) {
145		ctx := context.Background()
146		st := storetest.New(t)
147		user := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
148		test := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")

153	
154		gotTest, _ := st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, test.ID, 0)
155		if len(gotTest) != 1 || gotTest[0].Title != "in test" {

157		}
158		gotCC, _ := st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, chitChat.ID, 0)
159		if len(gotCC) != 1 || gotCC[0].Title != "in chitchat" {
160			t.Errorf("ChitChat board: got %+v, want one article 'in chitchat'", gotCC)
161		}
```

```text
96		deps, art := seedArticle(t)

99		m.height = 24 // viewportLines() = max(24-16, 5) = 8
100		m.scroll = 1

111		// fixture has 3 body lines, viewport=8 → max(0, 3-8) = 0
112		if got.scroll != 0 {

114		}
```

```text
1	package store_test
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"sync"
6		"testing"
7	
8		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store/storetest"
10	)
11	
12	func TestPushes_ScoreDeltas(t *testing.T) {
13		ctx := context.Background()
14		st := storetest.New(t)
15		alice := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
16		bob := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "bob", "")
17		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
18		art, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, alice.ID, alice.UserID, "t", "b")
19	
20		cases := []struct {
21			kind      store.PushKind
22			userID    int64
23			wantScore int64
24		}{
25			{store.PushKindPush, bob.ID, 1},
26			{store.PushKindPush, alice.ID, 2},
27			{store.PushKindBoo, bob.ID, 1},
28			{store.PushKindArrow, alice.ID, 1}, // arrow = 0 delta
29			{store.PushKindBoo, bob.ID, 0},
30			{store.PushKindBoo, bob.ID, -1},
31		}
32		for i, tc := range cases {
33			if _, err := st.Pushes().Create(ctx, art.ID, tc.userID, "x", tc.kind, "msg"); err != nil {
34				t.Fatalf("[%d] Create: %v", i, err)
35			}
36			fresh, err := st.Articles().GetByID(ctx, art.ID)
37			if err != nil {
38				t.Fatalf("[%d] GetByID: %v", i, err)
39			}
40			if fresh.RecommendScore != tc.wantScore {
41				t.Errorf("[%d] kind=%s: score = %d, want %d", i, tc.kind, fresh.RecommendScore, tc.wantScore)
42			}
43		}
44	}
45	
46	func TestPushes_ListPreservesInsertionOrder(t *testing.T) {
47		ctx := context.Background()
48		st := storetest.New(t)
49		alice := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
50		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
51		art, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, alice.ID, alice.UserID, "t", "b")
52	
53		bodies := []string{"first push", "second push", "third push"}
54		for _, body := range bodies {
55			if _, err := st.Pushes().Create(ctx, art.ID, alice.ID, alice.UserID, store.PushKindPush, body); err != nil {
56				t.Fatalf("Create: %v", err)
57			}
58		}
59		got, err := st.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, art.ID)
60		if err != nil {
61			t.Fatalf("ListByArticle: %v", err)
62		}
63		if len(got) != len(bodies) {
64			t.Fatalf("got %d, want %d", len(got), len(bodies))
65		}
66		for i, want := range bodies {
67			if got[i].Body != want {
68				t.Errorf("[%d].Body = %q, want %q", i, got[i].Body, want)
69			}
70		}
71	}
72	
73	func TestPushes_InvalidKind(t *testing.T) {
74		ctx := context.Background()
75		st := storetest.New(t)
76		alice := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
77		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
78		art, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, alice.ID, alice.UserID, "t", "b")
79		if _, err := st.Pushes().Create(ctx, art.ID, alice.ID, alice.UserID, "bogus", "x"); err == nil {
80			t.Error("expected error for invalid kind, got nil")
81		}
82	}
83	
84	// TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity asserts that N concurrent push inserts
85	// against the same article yield exactly N as the cached score — no lost
86	// updates from interleaved RMW. Run with `go test -race`.
87	func TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity(t *testing.T) {
88		ctx := context.Background()
89		st := storetest.New(t)
90		alice := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
91		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
92		art, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, alice.ID, alice.UserID, "t", "b")
93	
94		const N = 50
95		var wg sync.WaitGroup
96		wg.Add(N)
97		for i := 0; i < N; i++ {
98			go func() {
99				defer wg.Done()
100				if _, err := st.Pushes().Create(ctx, art.ID, alice.ID, alice.UserID, store.PushKindPush, "x"); err != nil {
101					t.Errorf("Create: %v", err)
102				}
103			}()
104		}
105		wg.Wait()
106	
107		fresh, err := st.Articles().GetByID(ctx, art.ID)
108		if err != nil {
109			t.Fatalf("GetByID: %v", err)
110		}
111		if fresh.RecommendScore != int64(N) {
112			t.Errorf("score = %d, want %d (lost update?)", fresh.RecommendScore, N)
113		}
114		pushes, _ := st.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, art.ID)
115		if len(pushes) != N {
116			t.Errorf("inserted %d, found %d in DB", N, len(pushes))
117		}
118	}
119
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/mail.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Mail struct {
11		ID            int64
12		ThreadID      int64
13		ParentID      sql.NullInt64
14		FromUserID    int64
15		FromUserIDStr string
16		ToUserID      int64
17		Subject       string
18		Body          string
19		ReadAt        sql.NullTime
20		CreatedAt     time.Time
21	}
22	
23	type MailRepo struct{ s *Store }
24	
25	var ErrMailNotFound = errors.New("mail not found")
26	
27	const mailColumns = `id, thread_id, parent_id, from_user_id, from_userid, to_user_id, subject, body, read_at, created_at`
28	
29	func scanMail(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Mail, error) {
30		m := &Mail{}
31		if err := row.Scan(
32			&m.ID, &m.ThreadID, &m.ParentID, &m.FromUserID, &m.FromUserIDStr,
33			&m.ToUserID, &m.Subject, &m.Body, &m.ReadAt, &m.CreatedAt,
34		); err != nil {
35			return nil, err
36		}
37		return m, nil
38	}
39	
40	// Insert creates a new mail. If parentID is nil, this is a thread root and
41	// thread_id is back-filled to the new row's own id under writeMu so the
42	// invariant `thread_id IS NOT NULL` holds for every read.
43	func (r *MailRepo) Insert(ctx context.Context, fromID int64, fromUserID string, toID int64, subject, body string, parentID *int64) (*Mail, error) {
44		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
45		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
46	
47		var threadID sql.NullInt64
48		var parent sql.NullInt64
49		if parentID != nil {
50			parent = sql.NullInt64{Int64: *parentID, Valid: true}
51			// Look up the parent's thread_id so the new reply joins the same thread.
52			row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT thread_id FROM mail WHERE id = ?`, *parentID)
53			var t sql.NullInt64
54			if err := row.Scan(&t); err != nil {
55				if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
56					return nil, ErrMailNotFound
57				}
58				return nil, err
59			}
60			threadID = t
61		}
62	
63		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
64			`INSERT INTO mail (thread_id, parent_id, from_user_id, from_userid, to_user_id, subject, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
65			threadID, parent, fromID, fromUserID, toID, subject, body,
66		)
67		if err != nil {
68			return nil, err
69		}
70		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
71		if err != nil {
72			return nil, err
73		}
74		if !threadID.Valid {
75			// Root mail — thread_id = id.
76			if _, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `UPDATE mail SET thread_id = ? WHERE id = ?`, id, id); err != nil {
77				return nil, err
78			}
79		}
80		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+mailColumns+` FROM mail WHERE id = ?`, id)
81		return scanMail(row)
82	}
83	
84	// ListInboxFor returns the recipient's inbox: unread first, then read,
85	// each group ordered newest-first. Mirrors WaterBalloonRepo.ListInboxFor.
86	func (r *MailRepo) ListInboxFor(ctx context.Context, toUserID int64, limit int) ([]*Mail, error) {
87		if limit <= 0 {
88			limit = 50
89		}
90		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
91			`SELECT `+mailColumns+` FROM mail WHERE to_user_id = ?
92			 ORDER BY (read_at IS NULL) DESC, id DESC LIMIT ?`,
93			toUserID, limit,
94		)
95		if err != nil {
96			return nil, err
97		}
98		defer rows.Close()
99		var out []*Mail
100		for rows.Next() {
101			m, err := scanMail(rows)
102			if err != nil {
103				return nil, err
104			}
105			out = append(out, m)
106		}
107		return out, rows.Err()
108	}
109	
110	// ListThread returns every mail in a thread, oldest first (chronological
111	// reading order — matches how the article-view-style thread screen reads).
112	func (r *MailRepo) ListThread(ctx context.Context, threadID int64) ([]*Mail, error) {
113		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
114			`SELECT `+mailColumns+` FROM mail WHERE thread_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,
115			threadID,
116		)
117		if err != nil {
118			return nil, err
119		}
120		defer rows.Close()
121		var out []*Mail
122		for rows.Next() {
123			m, err := scanMail(rows)
124			if err != nil {
125				return nil, err
126			}
127			out = append(out, m)
128		}
129		return out, rows.Err()
130	}
131	
132	func (r *MailRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Mail, error) {
133		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+mailColumns+` FROM mail WHERE id = ?`, id)
134		m, err := scanMail(row)
135		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
136			return nil, ErrMailNotFound
137		}
138		return m, err
139	}
140	
141	func (r *MailRepo) MarkRead(ctx context.Context, id int64) error {
142		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
143		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
144		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
145			`UPDATE mail SET read_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ? AND read_at IS NULL`, id,
146		)
147		return err
148	}
149	
150	func (r *MailRepo) MarkAllReadFor(ctx context.Context, toUserID int64) error {
151		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
152		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
153		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
154			`UPDATE mail SET read_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE to_user_id = ? AND read_at IS NULL`,
155			toUserID,
156		)
157		return err
158	}
159	
160	// CountUnreadFor returns the number of unread mails for the recipient,
161	// useful for the main-menu badge.
162	func (r *MailRepo) CountUnreadFor(ctx context.Context, toUserID int64) (int, error) {
163		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
164			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM mail WHERE to_user_id = ? AND read_at IS NULL`, toUserID,
165		)
166		var n int
167		if err := row.Scan(&n); err != nil {
168			return 0, err
169		}
170		return n, nil
171	}
172
```

 `./internal/tui/screen_post_compose.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textarea"
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
9		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
10	)
11	
12	const (
13		composeFocusTitle = iota
14		composeFocusBody
15	)
16	
17	type postComposeModel struct {
18		deps    Deps
19		boardID int64
20		title   textinput.Model
21		body    textarea.Model
22		focus   int
23		width   int
24		height  int
25		err     string
26	}
27	
28	func newPostComposeModel(deps Deps, boardID int64) postComposeModel {
29		ti := textinput.New()
30		ti.Placeholder = "標題 title"
31		ti.CharLimit = 64
32		ti.Width = 60
33		ti.Focus()
34	
35		ta := textarea.New()
36		ta.Placeholder = "內容 body…"
37		ta.CharLimit = 8000
38		ta.SetWidth(72)
39		ta.SetHeight(12)
40	
41		return postComposeModel{
42			deps:    deps,
43			boardID: boardID,
44			title:   ti,
45			body:    ta,
46			focus:   composeFocusTitle,
47		}
48	}
49	
50	func (m postComposeModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
51	
52	func (m postComposeModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
53		switch msg := msg.(type) {
54		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
55			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
56			bodyW := max(40, msg.Width-8)
57			bodyH := max(8, msg.Height-12)
58			m.body.SetWidth(bodyW)
59			m.body.SetHeight(bodyH)
60			m.title.Width = max(20, msg.Width-12)
61			return m, nil
62	
63		case tea.KeyMsg:
64			switch msg.String() {
65			case "esc":
66				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: m.boardID} }
67			case "tab":
68				m.toggleFocus()
69				return m, nil
70			case "ctrl+s":
71				return m.submit()
72			}
73		}
74	
75		var cmd tea.Cmd
76		if m.focus == composeFocusTitle {
77			// In title field, Enter advances to body.
78			if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok && k.String() == "enter" {
79				m.toggleFocus()
80				return m, nil
81			}
82			m.title, cmd = m.title.Update(msg)
83		} else {
84			m.body, cmd = m.body.Update(msg)
85		}
86		return m, cmd
87	}
88	
89	func (m *postComposeModel) toggleFocus() {
90		if m.focus == composeFocusTitle {
91			m.focus = composeFocusBody
92			m.title.Blur()
93			m.body.Focus()
94		} else {
95			m.focus = composeFocusTitle
96			m.body.Blur()
97			m.title.Focus()
98		}
99	}
100	
101	func (m postComposeModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
102		title := strings.TrimSpace(m.title.Value())
103		body := m.body.Value()
104		if title == "" {
105			m.err = "title is required"
106			return m, nil
107		}
108		if strings.TrimSpace(body) == "" {
109			m.err = "body is required"
110			return m, nil
111		}
112		ctx := context.Background()
113		u := m.deps.User
114		art, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Create(ctx, m.boardID, u.ID, u.UserID, title, body)
115		if err != nil {
116			m.err = err.Error()
117			return m, nil
118		}
119		if err := m.deps.Store.Users().IncrementPosts(ctx, u.ID); err != nil {
120			m.err = err.Error()
121			return m, nil
122		}
123		// Broadcast to other live sessions; recipients filter by BoardID and
124		// re-fetch the canonical list (mirrors the PushAddedMsg pattern).
125		if m.deps.Broker != nil {
126			m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, ArticleAddedMsg{
127				BoardID:      m.boardID,
128				ArticleID:    art.ID,
129				AuthorUserID: u.UserID,
130				Title:        art.Title,
131			})
132		}
133		return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: m.boardID} }
134	}
135	
136	func (m postComposeModel) View() string {
137		var b strings.Builder
138		b.WriteString("\n")
139		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 發表新文章 New Post "))
140		b.WriteString("\n\n")
141	
142		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title:") + "\n")
143		b.WriteString("  " + m.title.View() + "\n\n")
144	
145		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Body:") + "\n")
146		b.WriteString("  " + indent(m.body.View(), "  ") + "\n")
147	
148		if m.err != "" {
149			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
150		}
151		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab switch field · Enter (in title) → body · Ctrl+S send · Esc cancel"))
152		b.WriteString("\n")
153		return b.String()
154	}
155	
156	func indent(s, prefix string) string {
157		lines := strings.Split(s, "\n")
158		for i, ln := range lines {
159			if i == 0 {
160				continue
161			}
162			lines[i] = prefix + ln
163		}
164		return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
165	}
166
```

 `./internal/tui/screen_board_view.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7	
8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
11	)
12	
13	type boardViewModel struct {
14		deps     Deps
15		board    *store.Board
16		articles []*store.Article
17		cursor   int
18		width    int
19		height   int
20		loadErr  error
21	}
22	
23	func newBoardViewModel(deps Deps, boardID int64) boardViewModel {
24		ctx := context.Background()
25		board, err := deps.Store.Boards().GetByID(ctx, boardID)
26		if err != nil {
27			return boardViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
28		}
29		articles, err := deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, 100)
30		return boardViewModel{deps: deps, board: board, articles: articles, loadErr: err}
31	}
32	
33	func (m boardViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
34	
35	func (m boardViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
36		switch msg := msg.(type) {
37		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
38			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
39			return m, nil
40		case ArticleAddedMsg:
41			if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
42				ctx := context.Background()
43				if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, m.board.ID, 100); err == nil {
44					m.articles = arts
45				}
46			}
47			return m, nil
48		case tea.KeyMsg:
49			switch msg.String() {
50			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
51				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardList} }
52			case "Q":
53				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
54			case "up", "k", "[":
55				if m.cursor > 0 {
56					m.cursor--
57				}
58			case "down", "j", "]":
59				if m.cursor < len(m.articles)-1 {
60					m.cursor++
61				}
62			case "home", "g":
63				m.cursor = 0
64			case "end", "G":
65				if len(m.articles) > 0 {
66					m.cursor = len(m.articles) - 1
67				}
68			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
69				if len(m.articles) == 0 {
70					return m, nil
71				}
72				a := m.articles[m.cursor]
73				return m, func() tea.Msg {
74					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: a.ID, BoardID: a.BoardID}
75				}
76			case "p":
77				if m.board == nil {
78					return m, nil
79				}
80				return m, func() tea.Msg {
81					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenPostCompose, BoardID: m.board.ID}
82				}
83			}
84		}
85		return m, nil
86	}
87	
88	func (m boardViewModel) View() string {
89		var b strings.Builder
90		b.WriteString("\n")
91		if m.board != nil {
92			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 看板 %s · %s ", m.board.Name, m.board.Title)))
93		} else {
94			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 看板 "))
95		}
96		b.WriteString("\n\n")
97	
98		if m.loadErr != nil {
99			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
100			return b.String()
101		}
102		if len(m.articles) == 0 {
103			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(no articles yet — press p to write one)") + "\n")
104			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("p post · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"))
105			return b.String()
106		}
107	
108		const (
109			idxW    = 4
110			dateW   = 11
111			scoreW  = 4
112			authorW = 14
113		)
114		titleW := max(20, m.width-2-idxW-1-dateW-1-scoreW-1-authorW-2)
115		header := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
116			PadRight("#", idxW),
117			PadRight("Date", dateW),
118			PadRight("Sc.", scoreW),
119			PadRight("Author", authorW),
120			PadRight("Title", titleW),
121		)
122		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header) + "\n")
123	
124		for i, a := range m.articles {
125			score := ""
126			switch {
127			case a.RecommendScore >= 100:
128				score = "爆"
129			case a.RecommendScore > 0:
130				score = fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore)
131			case a.RecommendScore < 0:
132				score = "X" + fmt.Sprintf("%d", -a.RecommendScore)
133			}
134			row := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
135				PadRight(fmt.Sprintf("%d", i+1), idxW),
136				PadRight(a.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04"), dateW),
137				PadRight(score, scoreW),
138				PadRight(a.AuthorUserID, authorW),
139				Truncate(a.Title, titleW),
140			)
141			if i == m.cursor {
142				b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
143			} else {
144				b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")
145			}
146		}
147	
148		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · p post · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"))
149		b.WriteString("\n")
150		return b.String()
151	}
152
```

```go
package tui

import (
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store/storetest"
)

// Layer 1: Root.navigate intercepts compose-screen NavigateMsgs from
// guests with an ErrorMsg toast and leaves the current sub mounted.
func TestNavigate_GuestComposeBlocked(t *testing.T) {
	st := storetest.New(t)
	guest := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "guest", "Guest")
	if err := st.Users().SetRole(t.Context(), guest.ID, store.RoleGuest); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("SetRole: %v", err)
	}
	guest.Role = store.RoleGuest
	deps := Deps{Store: st, User: guest, Broker: chat.NewBroker()}
	r := NewRoot(deps)
	// Force the sub to a non-form screen so we know navigate didn't replace it.
	r.sub = newBoardListModel(deps)
	originalSub := r.sub

	for _, target := range []Screen{ScreenPostCompose, ScreenWBCompose, ScreenMailCompose, ScreenAdminUsers} {
		t.Run(screenName(target), func(t *testing.T) {
			model, cmd := r.Update(NavigateMsg{To: target})
			r2 := model.(Root)
			if r2.sub != originalSub {
				t.Errorf("sub was replaced for guest %v navigate; want unchanged", target)
			}
			msg := runCmd(cmd)
			err, ok := msg.(ErrorMsg)
			if !ok {
				t.Fatalf("got %T, want ErrorMsg", msg)
			}
			if err.Err == nil || !strings.Contains(err.Err.Error(), "唯讀") {
				t.Errorf("ErrorMsg = %v, want one mentioning 唯讀 (read-only)", err.Err)
			}
		})
	}
}

// Layer 1 / admin-only: navigate to ScreenAdminUsers as a non-admin
// (regular user) is rejected with a toast.
func TestNavigate_NonAdminAdminUsersBlocked(t *testing.T) {
	st := storetest.New(t)
	user := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "Alice")
	deps := Deps{Store: st, User: user, Broker: chat.NewBroker()}
	r := NewRoot(deps)
	r.sub = newBoardListModel(deps)
	originalSub := r.sub

	model, cmd := r.Update(NavigateMsg{To: ScreenAdminUsers})
	r2 := model.(Root)
	if r2.sub != originalSub {
		t.Error("non-admin should not be routed into admin screen")
	}
	msg := runCmd(cmd)
	err, ok := msg.(ErrorMsg)
	if !ok {
		t.Fatalf("got %T, want ErrorMsg", msg)
	}
	if err.Err == nil || !strings.Contains(err.Err.Error(), "管理員") {
		t.Errorf("ErrorMsg = %v, want one mentioning 管理員", err.Err)
	}
}

// Admins can navigate to ScreenAdminUsers freely. (Sanity: the guard
// should NOT fire for admins.) The screen itself doesn't exist yet — we
// just verify navigate doesn't emit an ErrorMsg here.
func TestNavigate_AdminCanReachAdminScreen(t *testing.T) {
	st := storetest.New(t)
	user := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "admin0", "Admin")
	if err := st.Users().SetRole(t.Context(), user.ID, store.RoleAdmin); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("SetRole: %v", err)
	}
	user.Role = store.RoleAdmin
	deps := Deps{Store: st, User: user, Broker: chat.NewBroker()}
	r := NewRoot(deps)
	r.sub = newBoardListModel(deps)

	_, cmd := r.Update(NavigateMsg{To: ScreenAdminUsers})
	msg := runCmd(cmd)
	if e, ok := msg.(ErrorMsg); ok {
		t.Errorf("admin nav to admin screen produced ErrorMsg: %v", e.Err)
	}
}

func screenName(s Screen) string {
	switch s {
	case ScreenPostCompose:
		return "PostCompose"
	case ScreenWBCompose:
		return "WBCompose"
	case ScreenMailCompose:
		return "MailCompose"
	case ScreenAdminUsers:
		return "AdminUsers"
	}
	return "?"
}

```

```go
package tui

import (
	"context"
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"strings"

	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
)

const adminUsersPageSize = 20

type adminUsersModel struct {
	deps    Deps
	users   []*store.User
	cursor  int
	page    int
	loadErr error
	toast   string
	width   int
	height  int
}

func newAdminUsersModel(deps Deps) adminUsersModel {
	m := adminUsersModel{deps: deps}
	m.reload()
	return m
}

func (m *adminUsersModel) reload() {
	users, err := m.deps.Store.Users().ListAll(context.Background(), adminUsersPageSize, m.page*adminUsersPageSize)
	m.users = users
	m.loadErr = err
	if m.cursor >= len(users) {
		m.cursor = max(0, len(users)-1)
	}
}

func (m adminUsersModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }

func (m adminUsersModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
	switch msg := msg.(type) {
	case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
		m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
		return m, nil
	case tea.KeyMsg:
		switch msg.String() {
		case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
		case "Q":
			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
		case "up", "k":
			if m.cursor > 0 {
				m.cursor--
			}
		case "down", "j":
			if m.cursor < len(m.users)-1 {
				m.cursor++
			}
		case "[":
			if m.page > 0 {
				m.page--
				m.cursor = 0
				m.reload()
			}
		case "]":
			// Try to advance; if reload comes back empty, snap back.
			m.page++
			m.cursor = 0
			m.reload()
			if len(m.users) == 0 && m.page > 0 {
				m.page--
				m.reload()
			}
		case "g":
			return m.applyRole(store.RoleGuest)
		case "u":
			return m.applyRole(store.RoleUser)
		case "M":
			return m.applyRole(store.RoleMod)
		case "a":
			return m.applyRole(store.RoleAdmin)
		}
	}
	return m, nil
}

func (m adminUsersModel) applyRole(target store.Role) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
	if len(m.users) == 0 || m.cursor < 0 || m.cursor >= len(m.users) {
		return m, nil
	}
	u := m.users[m.cursor]
	if u.Role == target {
		m.toast = fmt.Sprintf("%s 已是 %s", u.UserID, target)
		return m, nil
	}
	// Last-admin guard: refuse to demote the only remaining admin.
	if u.Role == store.RoleAdmin && target != store.RoleAdmin {
		count, err := m.deps.Store.Users().CountByRole(context.Background(), store.RoleAdmin)
		if err != nil {
			m.toast = err.Error()
			return m, nil
		}
		if count <= 1 {
			m.toast = "無法解除最後一名管理員 (cannot demote the last admin)"
			return m, nil
		}
	}
	if err := m.deps.Store.Users().SetRole(context.Background(), u.ID, target); err != nil {
		if errors.Is(err, store.ErrInvalidRole) {
			m.toast = "invalid role"
		} else {
			m.toast = err.Error()
		}
		return m, nil
	}
	m.toast = fmt.Sprintf("%s → %s", u.UserID, target)
	m.reload()
	return m, nil
}

func (m adminUsersModel) View() string {
	var b strings.Builder
	b.WriteString("\n")
	b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 使用者管理 Admin Users "))
	b.WriteString("\n\n")

	if m.loadErr != nil {
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
		return b.String()
	}
	if len(m.users) == 0 {
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(no users on this page)") + "\n")
		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("[ prev page · Esc back"))
		return b.String()
	}

	const (
		idW       = 5
		userIDW   = 14
		roleW     = 6
		flagW     = 12
		nicknameW = 16
	)
	header := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
		PadRight("id", idW),
		PadRight("user_id", userIDW),
		PadRight("role", roleW),
		PadRight("must_chg_pw", flagW),
		PadRight("nickname", nicknameW),
	)
	b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header) + "\n")

	for i, u := range m.users {
		flag := ""
		if u.MustChangePassword {
			flag = "yes"
		}
		row := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
			PadRight(fmt.Sprintf("%d", u.ID), idW),
			PadRight(u.UserID, userIDW),
			PadRight(string(u.Role), roleW),
			PadRight(flag, flagW),
			PadRight(Truncate(u.Nickname, nicknameW), nicknameW),
		)
		if i == m.cursor {
			b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
		} else {
			b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")
		}
	}

	b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("page %d", m.page+1)) + "\n")
	if m.toast != "" {
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleHighlight.Render(m.toast) + "\n")
	}
	b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(
		"j/k move · g/u/M/a set role (guest/user/Mod/admin) · [/] page · Q/Esc back",
	))
	b.WriteString("\n")
	return b.String()
}

```

```go
package tui

import (
	"context"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store/storetest"
)

// Build an admin-screen fixture with one admin (alice) and N other users
// in default role 'user'. Returns the model + the admin's User pointer.
func newAdminUsersFixture(t *testing.T, others int) (adminUsersModel, *store.Store, *store.User) {
	t.Helper()
	st := storetest.New(t)
	ctx := context.Background()
	admin := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "Alice")
	if err := st.Users().SetRole(ctx, admin.ID, store.RoleAdmin); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("SetRole admin: %v", err)
	}
	admin.Role = store.RoleAdmin
	for i := 0; i < others; i++ {
		_ = storetest.MustUser(t, st, peerName(i), "")
	}
	deps := Deps{Store: st, User: admin}
	return newAdminUsersModel(deps), st, admin
}

func peerName(i int) string {
	switch i {
	case 0:
		return "bob"
	case 1:
		return "carol"
	case 2:
		return "dave"
	}
	return "user_" + string(rune('a'+i))
}

func TestAdminUsers_LoadsListing(t *testing.T) {
	m, _, _ := newAdminUsersFixture(t, 2)
	if m.loadErr != nil {
		t.Fatalf("loadErr = %v", m.loadErr)
	}
	if len(m.users) != 3 {
		t.Errorf("got %d users, want 3 (alice + 2 peers)", len(m.users))
	}
}

func TestAdminUsers_PromoteToMod(t *testing.T) {
	m, st, admin := newAdminUsersFixture(t, 1)
	// Cursor onto bob (the second user; alice is first by id).
	m.cursor = 1
	if m.users[m.cursor].UserID != "bob" {
		t.Fatalf("cursor target = %q, want bob", m.users[m.cursor].UserID)
	}
	model, _ := m.Update(keyOf("M"))
	got := model.(adminUsersModel)
	bob, _ := st.Users().GetByUserID(context.Background(), "bob")
	if bob.Role != store.RoleMod {
		t.Errorf("bob.Role = %q, want mod", bob.Role)
	}
	if !strings.Contains(got.toast, "mod") {
		t.Errorf("toast = %q, want one mentioning 'mod'", got.toast)
	}
	_ = admin
}

func TestAdminUsers_LastAdminGuard(t *testing.T) {
	m, st, admin := newAdminUsersFixture(t, 1)
	// Cursor onto admin (alice).
	for i, u := range m.users {
		if u.ID == admin.ID {
			m.cursor = i
		}
	}
	// Try to demote alice from admin → user. Refused: she's the only admin.
	model, _ := m.Update(keyOf("u"))
	got := model.(adminUsersModel)
	stillAdmin, _ := st.Users().GetByID(context.Background(), admin.ID)
	if stillAdmin.Role != store.RoleAdmin {
		t.Errorf("alice was demoted despite being the last admin: now %q", stillAdmin.Role)
	}
	if !strings.Contains(got.toast, "管理員") && !strings.Contains(got.toast, "admin") {
		t.Errorf("toast = %q, want refusal message", got.toast)
	}

	// Promote bob to admin first, then alice→user must succeed.
	bob, _ := st.Users().GetByUserID(context.Background(), "bob")
	if err := st.Users().SetRole(context.Background(), bob.ID, store.RoleAdmin); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("seed bob admin: %v", err)
	}
	got.reload()
	model, _ = got.Update(keyOf("u"))
	got = model.(adminUsersModel)
	demoted, _ := st.Users().GetByID(context.Background(), admin.ID)
	if demoted.Role != store.RoleUser {
		t.Errorf("alice not demoted after seeding second admin: now %q", demoted.Role)
	}
}

func TestAdminUsers_BackKeys(t *testing.T) {
	m, _, _ := newAdminUsersFixture(t, 0)
	for _, key := range []string{"esc", "backspace", "left", "h", "Q"} {
		t.Run(key, func(t *testing.T) {
			_, cmd := m.Update(keyOf(key))
			msg := runCmd(cmd)
			nav, ok := msg.(NavigateMsg)
			if !ok {
				t.Fatalf("got %T, want NavigateMsg", msg)
			}
			if nav.To != ScreenMainMenu {
				t.Errorf("To = %v, want ScreenMainMenu", nav.To)
			}
		})
	}
}

func TestAdminUsers_NoOpWhenSameRole(t *testing.T) {
	m, _, _ := newAdminUsersFixture(t, 1)
	// Cursor onto bob (a regular user); pressing 'u' (the role he already has) toasts no-op.
	m.cursor = 1
	model, _ := m.Update(keyOf("u"))
	got := model.(adminUsersModel)
	if !strings.Contains(got.toast, "已是") && !strings.Contains(got.toast, "user") {
		t.Errorf("toast = %q, want no-op message mentioning the role", got.toast)
	}
}

// MainMenu admin entry presence is conditional on the user's role.
func TestMainMenu_AdminEntryConditional(t *testing.T) {
	regular := &store.User{ID: 1, UserID: "alice", Role: store.RoleUser}
	admin := &store.User{ID: 2, UserID: "admin0", Role: store.RoleAdmin}

	mRegular := newMainMenuModel(Deps{User: regular})
	for _, it := range mRegular.items {
		if it.to == ScreenAdminUsers {
			t.Error("regular user's main menu should not include the admin entry")
		}
	}

	mAdmin := newMainMenuModel(Deps{User: admin})
	found := false
	for _, it := range mAdmin.items {
		if it.to == ScreenAdminUsers {
			found = true
			break
		}
	}
	if !found {
		t.Error("admin's main menu should include the admin entry")
	}
}

```

```go
package store_test

import (
	"context"
	"errors"
	"testing"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store/storetest"
)

func TestUserRepo_SetRole_Round(t *testing.T) {
	st := storetest.New(t)
	ctx := context.Background()
	u := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "Alice")
	if u.Role != store.RoleUser {
		t.Fatalf("default Role = %q, want user", u.Role)
	}
	for _, r := range []store.Role{store.RoleMod, store.RoleAdmin, store.RoleGuest, store.RoleUser} {
		if err := st.Users().SetRole(ctx, u.ID, r); err != nil {
			t.Fatalf("SetRole(%s): %v", r, err)
		}
		fresh, _ := st.Users().GetByID(ctx, u.ID)
		if fresh.Role != r {
			t.Errorf("after SetRole(%s): got %q", r, fresh.Role)
		}
	}
}

func TestUserRepo_SetRole_Invalid(t *testing.T) {
	st := storetest.New(t)
	u := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
	if err := st.Users().SetRole(context.Background(), u.ID, store.Role("root")); !errors.Is(err, store.ErrInvalidRole) {
		t.Errorf("got %v, want ErrInvalidRole", err)
	}
}

func TestUserRepo_CountByRole(t *testing.T) {
	st := storetest.New(t)
	ctx := context.Background()
	a := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
	b := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "bob", "")
	c := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "carol", "")

	if err := st.Users().SetRole(ctx, a.ID, store.RoleAdmin); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("set admin: %v", err)
	}
	if err := st.Users().SetRole(ctx, b.ID, store.RoleMod); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("set mod: %v", err)
	}
	_ = c

	cases := map[store.Role]int{
		store.RoleAdmin: 1,
		store.RoleMod:   1,
		store.RoleUser:  1, // carol stays default 'user'
		store.RoleGuest: 0,
	}
	for role, want := range cases {
		got, err := st.Users().CountByRole(ctx, role)
		if err != nil {
			t.Fatalf("CountByRole(%s): %v", role, err)
		}
		if got != want {
			t.Errorf("count(%s) = %d, want %d", role, got, want)
		}
	}
}

func TestUserRepo_ListAll_Pagination(t *testing.T) {
	st := storetest.New(t)
	ctx := context.Background()
	// Seed 5 users.
	for _, name := range []string{"u1", "u2", "u3", "u4", "u5"} {
		_ = storetest.MustUser(t, st, name, "")
	}

	page1, err := st.Users().ListAll(ctx, 2, 0)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("page 1: %v", err)
	}
	if len(page1) != 2 {
		t.Errorf("page 1 size = %d, want 2", len(page1))
	}
	page2, _ := st.Users().ListAll(ctx, 2, 2)
	if len(page2) != 2 {
		t.Errorf("page 2 size = %d, want 2", len(page2))
	}
	page3, _ := st.Users().ListAll(ctx, 2, 4)
	if len(page3) != 1 {
		t.Errorf("page 3 size = %d, want 1 (last)", len(page3))
	}
	page4, _ := st.Users().ListAll(ctx, 2, 6)
	if len(page4) != 0 {
		t.Errorf("page 4 size = %d, want 0 (empty)", len(page4))
	}

	// Default limit kicks in when limit<=0.
	def, _ := st.Users().ListAll(ctx, 0, 0)
	if len(def) != 5 {
		t.Errorf("default limit page size = %d, want 5", len(def))
	}
}

func TestUserRepo_SetPassword_ClearsFlag(t *testing.T) {
	st := storetest.New(t)
	ctx := context.Background()
	u, err := st.Users().InsertSystemAccount(ctx, "admin", "$2a$12$placeholder.hash.value.for.test.placeholder.value", store.RoleAdmin, true)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("InsertSystemAccount: %v", err)
	}
	if !u.MustChangePassword {
		t.Fatal("seeded admin must have must_change_password=true")
	}
	if err := st.Users().SetPassword(ctx, u.ID, "$2a$12$new.value.for.test.placeholder.x"); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("SetPassword: %v", err)
	}
	fresh, _ := st.Users().GetByID(ctx, u.ID)
	if fresh.MustChangePassword {
		t.Error("must_change_password not cleared by SetPassword")
	}
}
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-30 00:47:54Z)**_

All 10 tasks shipped, full race suite green, binary builds with the new `-admin-password` flag.

## Summary

**Schema** — `0004_add_user_roles.sql` adds `users.role` (CHECK enum guest/user/mod/admin) and `users.must_change_password`. Existing rows pick up DEFAULT `'user'`.

**Auth** — `store.Role` with `AtLeast()`/`Valid()` rank helpers. New `ReservedUsernameGuest` + `ReservedUsernameAdmin`; `IsReservedUsername` blocks all three from `Register`. `MustChangePasswordRemoteBlocked` refuses bootstrapped admin from non-loopback while the flag is set. `auth.HashPassword`/`VerifyPasswordHash`/`ValidatePassword` exposed so the TUI can rotate without re-importing bcrypt.

**Bootstrap** — `auth.SeedSystemAccounts` (called from `cmd/sshbbs/main.go`) idempotently creates `admin` (default `admin`/`admin`, `must_change_password=1`, overridable via `-admin-password`) and `guest` (sentinel hash `"!"`).

**SSH callback** — three-way switch on `ctx.User()`: `new` → register, `guest` → short-circuit to seeded row, otherwise bcrypt + remote-admin block. Guest sessions skip broker registration so multi-session `ssh guest@…` doesn't collide on user.ID.

**First-login change** — `screen_password_change.go` (form-style, no h/l) routed via new `Deps.MustChangePassword`. Refreshes the in-memory `*store.User` so the cleared flag propagates.

**Article delete** — `ArticleRepo.Delete(id, requesterID, role)` under writeMu, `ErrPermissionDenied` for non-owner non-mod. UI: `D` → y/N confirm, navigates to board view on success.

**Push delete** — `PushRepo.Delete` reverts `recommend_score` symmetrically inside writeMu+tx; 50× concurrent-delete race test mirrors the create canary. Inline cursor (`p`/`P`) — `D` targets cursored push when set, otherwise the article. Cursor clamps to -1 when last push is deleted.

**Read-only** — `Root.navigate` rejects compose/admin screens for guest with `ErrorMsg`; help-line cosmetics hide write keys per role; admin-only screen has its own non-admin guard for defense in depth.

**Admin screen** — `screen_admin_users.go` (paginated, j/k cursor, `g/u/M/a` keys, `[`/`]` paging) with last-admin guard via `CountByRole`. Main menu adds **管理 Admin** entry only when `deps.User.Role == RoleAdmin`; numeric jump shortcut now `1-6`.

**Tests** — added in every layer: `role_test.go`, `users_test.go`, `policy_test.go`, `seed_test.go`, `root_test.go`, `screen_password_change_test.go`, `screen_admin_users_test.go`, plus extensions to `articles_test.go`, `pushes_test.go`, `screen_article_view_test.go`, `auth_test.go`. All run under `-race`.

**TODO.md** — shipped as a Done entry; follow-ups filed: audit log (P2/S), per-board moderators (P2/M), banned/suspended flags (P2/S), GDPR self-service deletion (P3/M). Cross-linked the existing P2 soft-delete entry to the moderation work.

To smoke-test interactively: `make db-reset && make run`, then `ssh admin@localhost -p 2222` (password `admin` → password-change screen) and `ssh guest@localhost -p 2222` (any password → read-only main menu).

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-30 02:49:04Z)**_

短答：不是 SSH 函式庫的硬限制，是我們**沒有打開** SSH 的「none 認證」分支。可以做到 `ssh guest@host` 完全不需要密碼提示。讓我直接幫你修。

技術說明：
- SSH 協議（RFC 4252 §5.2）有個 `none` method — client 連線時會先試 `none`，server 可以 accept 或 list 其他 methods。
- 底層 `golang.org/x/crypto/ssh` 已經支援 `NoClientAuth + NoClientAuthCallback` 做 per-username 判斷（[server.go:101–107](file:///Users/daviddwlee84/go/pkg/mod/golang.org/x/crypto@v0.37.0/ssh/server.go#L101)）。
- 但 `charmbracelet/ssh` 包了一層，預設只在「完全沒設任何 handler」時才打開 `NoClientAuth`（[server.go:143](file:///Users/daviddwlee84/go/pkg/mod/github.com/charmbracelet/ssh@v0.0.0-20250826160808-ebfa259c7309/server.go#L143)）。我們有 `PasswordHandler`，所以這條路被關掉了。
- 解法：用 `ServerConfigCallback` 自己塞一份 `*gossh.ServerConfig{NoClientAuth: true, NoClientAuthCallback: ...}` 進去，callback 只對 `guest` 放行。

實作 — 只需動 `internal/server/server.go` 一個檔案：

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-30 02:49:05Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/server/server.go`

```text
1	package server
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5	
6		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
7		"github.com/charmbracelet/log"
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/ssh"
9		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish"
10		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/activeterm"
11		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/bubbletea"
12		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/logging"
13		"github.com/muesli/termenv"
14	
15		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
16		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
17		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/tui"
18	)
19	
20	type Config struct {
21		Addr    string
22		HostKey string
23	}
24	
25	func New(cfg Config, st *store.Store, broker *chat.Broker) (*ssh.Server, error) {
26		return wish.NewServer(
27			wish.WithAddress(cfg.Addr),
28			wish.WithHostKeyPath(cfg.HostKey),
29			wish.WithPasswordAuth(PasswordAuth(st)),
30			wish.WithMiddleware(
31				bubbletea.MiddlewareWithProgramHandler(makeProgramHandler(st, broker), termenv.ANSI256),
32				activeterm.Middleware(),
33				logging.Middleware(),
34			),
35		)
36	}
37	
38	// makeProgramHandler is the wish ProgramHandler. It owns *tea.Program
39	// creation so we can hand the pointer to the chat broker for live message
40	// delivery, and arrange Unregister when the SSH session ends.
41	func makeProgramHandler(st *store.Store, broker *chat.Broker) bubbletea.ProgramHandler {
42		return func(sess ssh.Session) *tea.Program {
43			ctx := sess.Context()
44	
45			// Register-mode: SSH user was "new". No DB user, no broker registration.
46			if reg, ok := ctx.Value(ctxKeyRegister).(bool); ok && reg {
47				deps := tui.Deps{Store: st, Broker: broker, IsRegister: true}
48				m := tui.NewRoot(deps)
49				return tea.NewProgram(m, append(bubbletea.MakeOptions(sess), tea.WithAltScreen())...)
50			}
51	
52			uid, _ := ctx.Value(ctxKeyUserID).(int64)
53			if uid == 0 {
54				log.Warn("session has no user_id; rejecting", "remote", sess.RemoteAddr())
55				return nil
56			}
57			user, err := st.Users().GetByID(context.Background(), uid)
58			if err != nil {
59				log.Error("load user", "id", uid, "err", err)
60				return nil
61			}
62	
63			deps := tui.Deps{Store: st, User: user, Broker: broker}
64			if user.MustChangePassword {
65				deps.MustChangePassword = true
66			}
67			m := tui.NewRoot(deps)
68			p := tea.NewProgram(m, append(bubbletea.MakeOptions(sess), tea.WithAltScreen())...)
69	
70			// Guests are read-only spectators. We deliberately don't register
71			// them in the broker — multiple `ssh guest@...` clients would
72			// collide on the same user.ID, they can't reply to water balloons,
73			// and they don't appear in the online list.
74			if user.Role == store.RoleGuest {
75				log.Info("guest session", "host", deps.User.UserID)
76				return p
77			}
78	
79			// Hook session into the broker so other users can send to us.
80			cs := &chat.Session{UserID: user.ID, UserIDStr: user.UserID, Program: p}
81			broker.Register(cs)
82			log.Info("session registered", "user", user.UserID, "online", len(broker.OnlineList()))
83	
84			// Drain unread water balloons from DB and replay them as toasts.
85			// We do this *before* the program starts so the messages land in
86			// the model's first Update(); tea.Program.Send buffers if needed.
87			go func() {
88				unread, err := st.WaterBalloons().ListUnreadFor(context.Background(), user.ID)
89				if err != nil {
90					log.Warn("drain unread WBs", "user", user.UserID, "err", err)
91					return
92				}
93				for _, w := range unread {
94					p.Send(tui.WBIncomingMsg{ID: w.ID, FromUserID: w.FromUserIDStr, Body: w.Body})
95					_ = st.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), w.ID)
96				}
97			}()
98	
99			// On session end, unregister so we stop trying to deliver to a dead Program.
100			go func() {
101				<-ctx.Done()
102				broker.Unregister(user.ID, p)
103				log.Info("session unregistered", "user", user.UserID, "online", len(broker.OnlineList()))
104			}()
105	
106			return p
107		}
108	}
109
```
</details></tool-use>

```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add user permission system: admin / mod / user / guest

Migration 0004 adds users.role (CHECK enum) and must_change_password.
Six commits' worth of work in one batch — all tests run under -race.

- store.Role with rank-ordered AtLeast()/Valid(); auth.SeedSystemAccounts
  bootstraps admin (default 'admin'/'admin' + must_change_password=1,
  overridable via -admin-password) and guest (sentinel hash "!"). admin
  remote login is refused while the flag is set
  (MustChangePasswordRemoteBlocked), so the source-code default password
  can't be cracked from non-loopback addresses

- SSH `none` auth via ServerConfigCallback lets `ssh guest@host` and
  `ssh new@host` connect with zero password prompts; non-reserved users
  still go through the password callback

- screen_password_change.go (form-style, no h/l) routed by new
  Deps.MustChangePassword; clears the flag in DB and refreshes the
  in-memory User pointer

- screen_admin_users.go: paginated list (UserRepo.ListAll), j/k cursor,
  g/u/M/a role keys, last-admin guard via UserRepo.CountByRole; main
  menu adds 管理 Admin only when deps.User.Role == RoleAdmin

- ArticleRepo.Delete and PushRepo.Delete with owner-or-mod permission
  check returning ErrPermissionDenied. Article view: D + y/N confirm;
  push cursor (p/P) targets a push before D when set, else the article.
  Push delete reverts recommend_score under writeMu+tx; 50× concurrent-
  delete race test mirrors the existing create canary

- Read-only enforcement: Root.navigate intercepts compose / admin
  screens for guests with ErrorMsg toast; help-line cosmetics hide
  write keys; guests skip broker registration so multi-session
  collisions on user.ID don't matter

- TODO.md: shipped Done entry; new P2/P3 follow-ups for audit log,
  per-board moderators, banned/suspended flags, GDPR self-deletion;
  cross-linked the existing soft-delete entry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git status
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/server/server.go`

```text
1	package server
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6	
7		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/log"
9		"github.com/charmbracelet/ssh"
10		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish"
11		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/activeterm"
12		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/bubbletea"
13		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/logging"
14		"github.com/muesli/termenv"
15		gossh "golang.org/x/crypto/ssh"
16	
17		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/auth"
18		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
19		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
20		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/tui"
21	)
22	
23	type Config struct {
24		Addr    string
25		HostKey string
26	}
27	
28	func New(cfg Config, st *store.Store, broker *chat.Broker) (*ssh.Server, error) {
29		return wish.NewServer(
30			wish.WithAddress(cfg.Addr),
31			wish.WithHostKeyPath(cfg.HostKey),
32			wish.WithPasswordAuth(PasswordAuth(st)),
33			withReservedNoneAuth(),
34			wish.WithMiddleware(
35				bubbletea.MiddlewareWithProgramHandler(makeProgramHandler(st, broker), termenv.ANSI256),
36				activeterm.Middleware(),
37				logging.Middleware(),
38			),
39		)
40	}
41	
42	// withReservedNoneAuth enables SSH "none" authentication for the two
43	// reserved usernames where a password is meaningless: `guest` (read-only
44	// spectator) and `new` (in-TUI registration). Other usernames still go
45	// through the password callback.
46	//
47	// charmbracelet/ssh only sets NoClientAuth=true when no other handlers
48	// are configured (see its server.go config()), so we override its
49	// ServerConfigCallback to force NoClientAuth=true with a per-username
50	// callback. The library still adds HostKey + PasswordCallback on top of
51	// our config, so password auth keeps working for everyone else.
52	//
53	// Effect: `ssh guest@host -p 2222` and `ssh new@host -p 2222` connect
54	// with no password prompt. `ssh alice@host -p 2222` still prompts.
55	func withReservedNoneAuth() ssh.Option {
56		return func(s *ssh.Server) error {
57			s.ServerConfigCallback = func(ctx ssh.Context) *gossh.ServerConfig {
58				return &gossh.ServerConfig{
59					NoClientAuth: true,
60					NoClientAuthCallback: func(conn gossh.ConnMetadata) (*gossh.Permissions, error) {
61						switch conn.User() {
62						case auth.ReservedUsernameGuest, auth.ReservedUsernameNew:
63							return nil, nil
64						}
65						return nil, errors.New("auth required")
66					},
67				}
68			}
69			return nil
70		}
71	}
72	
73	// makeProgramHandler is the wish ProgramHandler. It owns *tea.Program
74	// creation so we can hand the pointer to the chat broker for live message
75	// delivery, and arrange Unregister when the SSH session ends.
76	func makeProgramHandler(st *store.Store, broker *chat.Broker) bubbletea.ProgramHandler {
77		return func(sess ssh.Session) *tea.Program {
78			ctx := sess.Context()
79	
80			// Register-mode: SSH user was "new". No DB user, no broker registration.
81			// `none` auth bypasses our PasswordHandler, so ctxKeyRegister isn't set
82			// when a client connects via the none-auth path — fall back to inspecting
83			// sess.User() directly.
84			reg, _ := ctx.Value(ctxKeyRegister).(bool)
85			if reg || sess.User() == auth.ReservedUsernameNew {
86				deps := tui.Deps{Store: st, Broker: broker, IsRegister: true}
87				m := tui.NewRoot(deps)
88				return tea.NewProgram(m, append(bubbletea.MakeOptions(sess), tea.WithAltScreen())...)
89			}
90	
91			uid, _ := ctx.Value(ctxKeyUserID).(int64)
92			if uid == 0 {
93				// SSH `none` auth path: NoClientAuthCallback succeeded for
94				// guest but didn't run our PasswordHandler, so user_id was
95				// never stashed. Resolve the guest row here. Any other
96				// username with uid==0 means a misconfiguration — reject.
97				if sess.User() == auth.ReservedUsernameGuest {
98					guest, err := st.Users().GetByUserID(context.Background(), auth.ReservedUsernameGuest)
99					if err == nil && guest.Role == store.RoleGuest {
100						uid = guest.ID
101					}
102				}
103				if uid == 0 {
104					log.Warn("session has no user_id; rejecting", "remote", sess.RemoteAddr(), "user", sess.User())
105					return nil
106				}
107			}
108			user, err := st.Users().GetByID(context.Background(), uid)
109			if err != nil {
110				log.Error("load user", "id", uid, "err", err)
111				return nil
112			}
113	
114			deps := tui.Deps{Store: st, User: user, Broker: broker}
115			if user.MustChangePassword {
116				deps.MustChangePassword = true
117			}
118			m := tui.NewRoot(deps)
119			p := tea.NewProgram(m, append(bubbletea.MakeOptions(sess), tea.WithAltScreen())...)
120	
121			// Guests are read-only spectators. We deliberately don't register
122			// them in the broker — multiple `ssh guest@...` clients would
123			// collide on the same user.ID, they can't reply to water balloons,
124			// and they don't appear in the online list.
125			if user.Role == store.RoleGuest {
126				log.Info("guest session", "host", deps.User.UserID)
127				return p
128			}
129	
130			// Hook session into the broker so other users can send to us.
131			cs := &chat.Session{UserID: user.ID, UserIDStr: user.UserID, Program: p}
132			broker.Register(cs)
133			log.Info("session registered", "user", user.UserID, "online", len(broker.OnlineList()))
134	
135			// Drain unread water balloons from DB and replay them as toasts.
136			// We do this *before* the program starts so the messages land in
137			// the model's first Update(); tea.Program.Send buffers if needed.
138			go func() {
139				unread, err := st.WaterBalloons().ListUnreadFor(context.Background(), user.ID)
140				if err != nil {
141					log.Warn("drain unread WBs", "user", user.UserID, "err", err)
142					return
143				}
144				for _, w := range unread {
145					p.Send(tui.WBIncomingMsg{ID: w.ID, FromUserID: w.FromUserIDStr, Body: w.Body})
146					_ = st.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), w.ID)
147				}
148			}()
149	
150			// On session end, unregister so we stop trying to deliver to a dead Program.
151			go func() {
152				<-ctx.Done()
153				broker.Unregister(user.ID, p)
154				log.Info("session unregistered", "user", user.UserID, "online", len(broker.OnlineList()))
155			}()
156	
157			return p
158		}
159	}
160
```
</details></tool-use>

Commit feature batch (without none-auth)

```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add user permission system: admin / mod / user / guest

Migration 0004 adds users.role (CHECK enum) and must_change_password.
All tests run under -race.

- store.Role with rank-ordered AtLeast()/Valid(); auth.SeedSystemAccounts
  bootstraps admin (default 'admin'/'admin' + must_change_password=1,
  overridable via -admin-password) and guest (sentinel hash "!"). Admin
  remote login is refused while the flag is set
  (MustChangePasswordRemoteBlocked), so the source-code default password
  can't be cracked from non-loopback addresses

- screen_password_change.go (form-style, no h/l) routed by new
  Deps.MustChangePassword; clears the flag in DB and refreshes the
  in-memory User pointer

- screen_admin_users.go: paginated list (UserRepo.ListAll), j/k cursor,
  g/u/M/a role keys, last-admin guard via UserRepo.CountByRole; main
  menu adds 管理 Admin only when deps.User.Role == RoleAdmin

- ArticleRepo.Delete and PushRepo.Delete with owner-or-mod permission
  check returning ErrPermissionDenied. Article view: D + y/N confirm;
  push cursor (p/P) targets a push before D when set, else the article.
  Push delete reverts recommend_score under writeMu+tx; 50× concurrent-
  delete race test mirrors the existing create canary

- Read-only enforcement: Root.navigate intercepts compose / admin
  screens for guests with ErrorMsg toast; help-line cosmetics hide
  write keys; guests skip broker registration so multi-session
  collisions on user.ID don't matter

- TODO.md: shipped Done entry; new P2/P3 follow-ups for audit log,
  per-board moderators, banned/suspended flags, GDPR self-deletion;
  cross-linked the existing soft-delete entry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -3
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Commit none-auth bypass for guest + new

```bash
git add internal/server/server.go && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Skip password prompt for `guest` and `new` via SSH none-auth

Both reserved usernames had no meaningful password (guest's hash is the
sentinel "!"; new's password was always accepted by the callback), but
the OpenSSH client still prompted because the server only advertised
`password` as an auth method. Configure NoClientAuth + NoClientAuthCallback
via charmbracelet/ssh's ServerConfigCallback escape hatch so the SSH
`none` method authenticates these two usernames at the protocol level.

charmbracelet/ssh only sets NoClientAuth=true when *no* handlers are
configured — we have a PasswordHandler, so the library leaves it false.
Overriding ServerConfigCallback returns a pre-populated *gossh.ServerConfig
that the library augments (HostKey, PasswordCallback) without touching the
NoClientAuth fields. Per-username gating in the callback keeps password
auth strictly required for every other user.

makeProgramHandler grew two fallbacks since `none` skips PasswordHandler
and therefore never stashes ctxKeyRegister / ctxKeyUserID:
- sess.User() == "new"   → register mode
- sess.User() == "guest" + uid==0 → look up the seeded guest row

Verified end-to-end with BatchMode SSH against a fresh server:
  guest → "Authenticated using \"none\""
  new   → "Authenticated using \"none\""
  alice → "Permission denied (password)" (still password-required)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -4
```
